approaches before going over the curriculum we must first discuss how to best teach all of the following methods outlined below are what would i would suggest be use to teach the students trying to use just one or even just two of these would not be enough and would compromise the students learning and education teaching methods project based learning pbl in pbl students work on a project over an extended period which could be a week or a month to respond to a complex question problem or challenge the projects are usually multi disciplinary and require students to apply what they ve learned in a practical manner this allows them to see the immediate applicability of their learning inquiry based learning this is a form of active learning that starts by posing questions problems or scenarios rather than simply presenting established facts or portraying a smooth path to knowledge students are involved in the construction of their learning they engage with the material participate in the class and collaborate with each other gamification incorporating elements of game design in education can make learning fun and engaging this can involve point systems leaderboards badges or other game mechanics experiential learning this method involves learning by doing and reflecting on the experience it can include internships study abroad programs field trips laboratory experiments or any other hands on learning experiences flipped classroom in a flipped classroom students review lecture materials at home and do their homework in class where they can ask for help as they practice new skills and apply new knowledge this allows teachers to spend class time helping students apply what they ve learned and coaching them as they work through challenges cross disciplinary projects by integrating different disciplines into a single project you can make the learning experience more holistic and interconnected much like how the knowledge of different magical disciplines before we re done one last thing must be covered how to assess a student s growth because science shows that paper tests are not suited for the task there are many innovative ways to assess student understanding and skills without relying solely on traditional exams the methods that could be used include assessment methods portfolios students could compile portfolios of their work which could include code they ve written projects they ve completed or essays they ve written a portfolio allows students to demonstrate their learning process their progress over time and their ability to apply session every day would ideally give students a break from traditional instruction and allow them to process and manage their learnings this can be in the form of group discussions independent reflection time or guided activities for planning and organizing their work', 'here s part before i start a lot of you have been sending me your essays and a lot of them fall into one of the patterns i noted in the first thread or some combination of the patterns i m going to try to address how you can troubleshoot them on your own in this thread at the end if you haven t please read some guides by u scholargrade or read some of the comments on the first thread i feel as though most of the issues you all are having are already addressed by the guides scholargrade has posted as i must preface once again i am not an expert on writing personal statements please take whatever i say with a grain of salt whatever kind of advice i express are things that i would have done differently if i were to write an essay there is plenty of advice from actual experts on this sub so please look at their guides before you make any fast conclusions the merely ok essay everytime i see a chanceme and the student rates their own essay they always rate it as i m sure most of you have noticed most essays are in fact not most of the essays i ve read that weren t super bad as in too much narrative or whatever were just average at best these essays usually have some common insights with a common topic with basic writing for instance i read an essay that was about learning how to be confident there is certainly nothing wrong with this kind of topic as it s mainly how you execute your topic that will make it bad average or good however this essay i read was very basic it described how at first they just didn t like to talk suddenly started to talk to people and are now comfortable in their own skin the writing itself was pretty bland and there was nothing memorable about the prose diction or tone the insights they made were while significant to them commonplace to me because i ve read other essays prior that talked about the same exact thing as a result it didn t really move the needle for me nothing stood out in my opinion there are a couple of ways to fix this one way would be to lead your reader somewhere unfamiliar unexpected for the confidence topic i would have liked to see more of how the student applied their confidence in situations that are perhaps not commonly talked about maybe instead of writing about how you were confident talking to people or in front of a crowd you can dive into how your confidence pushes you to do unorthodox things like exploring new routes in hikes or something obviously my example sucks because i don t have that kind of experience of confidence leading to something uncommon but i hope it illustrates my point nonetheless by taking your reader someone unfamiliar unexpected i feel as though it s more memorable you won t be that kid that was like four other students who had built confidence and could now talk to people you are now that confident kid that not only talks to people but uses that confidence in other aspects of their life that aren t simply confined to speaking of course make it flow i also want to note that thinking of unexpected avenues is hard don t expect to just have the idea in your head in a minute another way to make your essay better than average is to leverage the emotions behind something there was some post from a former ao on this sub i can t remember who but if any of yall can remember please link it below who talked about making the reader feel something i completely agree with this when i can feel something after i read an essay like empathy anxiety laughter the essay feels more real and genuine i can actually get more of a sense of who the person is while also being able to remember their essay or at least how i felt after i read their essay there are tons of ways to do this from prose to diction to your narrative i want to caution though this kind of writing should be genuine it s very easy for this in my opinion type of writing to come off as forced if you re writing a narrative really think about the events that happened did you feel anything what did you feel don t mistake this to mean that i m telling you to be flowery or descriptive make sure whatever emotional tug you have in your essay helps to advance who you are plot insights emotion just for the sake of emotion can ruin the flow and come off as forced the third way i would probably make an average essay better would be by changing the prose diction phrases tone i ve seen so many essays that are just bland the writing is as if my professor wrote it for an academic journal i just get super bored super tired of reading and sometimes the paragraphs are so long that i get lost and give up reading because i don t know where the hell i left off so how you do fix this well i think it s a bit complicated because it relates back to your voice in an essay if your voice isn t the type to be creative and daring in their diction prose tone then don t do this it ll come off as an insincere however if you do have a rich personal voice don t be afraid to use it for example instead of saying something like i m a wild person you can say i m a human exclamation mark again perhaps not the best example but i hope it illustrates my point when you are purposeful with your diction or tone or whatever it tends to jump out on paper this is also another way to convey passion i ve seen a lot of sentences where they go something like i grew infinitely more passionate about cs after xyz but if you replaced that sentence with something a bit more daring and bold and creative it feels more genuine and sincere again do not do this if it isn t you if you re the type of person who just cannot write creatively then don t i also want to caution that there s a fine line between flowery and creative flowery to me is just a bunch of fluff with no real meaning behind it other than to just showcase you know how to describe something in a more than needed way creative is the process of using her head and being purposeful with what you say such that it can convey the meaning you want it to there are other ways to make an ok essay better but this is what i would do don t take this to mean you need to integrate all these strategies figure out what is natural to you the computer science essay most of this sub is trying to get into cs ok i get that but a ton of you have written the same thing you built a website you loved the beauty and intricacy of your code cs is your biggest problem that you love to solve you competed in some competition and learned a lot about cs and how much more you have to learn and then some rehash of some ecs you do these are all great sentiments but to me are overplayed this ties back to the common topic average essay i was talking about but in the context of cs it just doesn t move the needle for me most students are learning how to code on their own and that s great but when of you write about building a website and how much you love code because of it it s really hard to differentiate any of you the main thing that i want to ask is this what else about code do you love besides the coding itself is there a deeper meaning as to why you code maybe you re looking into the socioeconomic implications that tech has and you re seeking a way to ensure that these kinds of implications don t ruin society maybe you love to code because it gives you the chance to be creative and original and you write about how it is creative for you and why it s important maybe you code because you re passionate about saving endangered species and you want to identify them with ai to prevent them from being hunted or whatever all that i m saying is that you should find something beyond just building a website because it s starting to become trite questions i ve received from some of you and how i would troubleshoot them do i convey enough about myself in my essay there s no real easy way to answer this without reading your essay there was some fantastic advice i read from u peteymit where he suggested that you should be learning something new about yourself in your essay this is something i always advocate with a student i m currently working with you should ask yourself the same questions am i learning something new about myself am i learning anything about myself at all either explicitly stated or implied if you re finding that essay doesn t reveal anything about your values traits ideas thoughts motivations personality one essay doesn t need to have all these things because again it may fall flat here s what i recommend start with i statements when you start with i right off the bat there s no way you can t make it about yourself i also recommend just talking outloud either to yourself or someone else talk about yourself get used to it a lot of students really struggle with talking about themselves but there s no other way around this if you can t talk about yourself either in your essay or in person how am i going to figure out anything about you when i read your application record yourself talking then listen to the recording notice anything that sticks out that might be worth diving into talk more about it figure out who you are what you love what you value how you think where you re going in life these are things that are good starting points to figure out what you want to convey in an essay i also find that asking yourself the questions like what s the point what is the purpose of this sentence as it relates to my story me after every line is helpful if you find that you can t really answer those questions after going line by line then maybe it s time to go back to the drawing board and think about what you want to convey again is my topic of disabilities abuse sexual assault mental health etc a sob story personally i think it s less about the topic you write about and more so about how you write it that makes it a sob story i ll give you an example i have a friend who is disabled he s paralyzed from the hip down we once had a very serious conversation about how he views life and what he told me was very moving he said this not exactly verbatim but close enough i have two choices i can see the world as a scam and curse at everyone because i m bitter that s actually the easiest thing to do i hate how i can t play basketball anymore i hate how it takes extra time for me to get ready in the morning just because i can t walk but fuck that my height might have been cut in half but my life is still full so fuck all the weeping the sorrow the pity the i feel bad cut all of that out if i want to go out and have fun with my boys i m going do it i may not be able to play basketball anymore but that doesn t stop my love for the sport i still hate the lakers nothing has changed hell i can still shoot the ball and that s more than enough is it harder yeah but i like that i like the challenge throw me in hell and i ll find my way back to heaven i m disabled yeah but i m a warrior too now compare that back to your sob story notice the difference my friend spent less time talking about his disabilities and how sad he is and more so about how he wants to challenge the world he s not afraid he s ready he s willing he wants to live and the disability is not stopping him there s a completely different connotation you get from this if he wrote his essay about this topic i can guarantee you that of it would probably be about his disability and of it would be about his dreams his aspirations how he sees the world how he thinks differently because of his disability how he s ready to fight despite his accident so my advice is to really think about your sob story and focus on how you have changed read your sob story essay and try to pick up if you get a negative connotation if you do scratch it and write about what you ve learned how you ve grown where you re going is writing about my ec in my essay bad i would say yes if you re rehashing whatever you wrote in your extracurricular section if you wrote in your ec section how you fundraised k don t repeat it in your essay when you plug it like that it just makes you seem desperate if you re expanding upon your ec answering the heavy question of why you do what you do then i think that s fair game i m open to answering more questions in this thread if there are any again i m not an expert just a college student procrastinating on projects and labs i should be doing hope this helps']

Name: 0_code_replace_ai_jobs
Representative_Docs: ['it depends on how good you can get at what you do do not worry about ai its only a tool lever that is supposed to increase developer productivity the ones screaming ai will replace our jobs are the one who do not fundamentally understand that ai is just a marketing term and llms will not replace developers or are the one who do not bring much to the table to begin with these are the ones who will eventually lose their jobs not because ai will replace them but because skilled developers that have been carrying their dead weight can perform the menial tasks these developers work on without losing productivity by leveraging ai lazy unskilled developers jr developers i ve worked with a lot of very talented jr developers in my career its all about work ethic and passion the art', 'i m a full stack engineer with years of experience and a cs background i wrote a first draft of this reply then asked ai to help polish the flow and make it more readable that alone kind of proves the point when used intentionally ai can be a huge multiplier i use ai daily and it has accelerated my work by orders of magnitude if you re new here s my main advice don t just copy paste understand ask the ai to explain code line by line if needed keep your functions small it makes it easier for both you and the ai to work within focused contexts i work across a lot of different stacks including frameworks languages and apis so ai helps me switch gears quickly i focus on understanding what the code does and why it s structured that way and i let ai fill in the smaller details like syntax or repetitive boilerplate how do you stay intentional with ai tools i don t use code i don t understand it s usually faster to read and make sense of code than to write it from scratch if i don t know what i need i ll have a conversation with the ai to figure it out if it suggests indexing a database in a certain way i ask why if the explanation makes sense great if it doesn t then either it s hallucinating or i need to level up my understanding either way i treat that as a learning checkpoint do you treat ai output as a first draft or something to deeply understand it depends if i m starting a new project i ll describe what i m trying to build discuss trade offs and get a scaffolded first pass sometimes i build on that sometimes i throw it out and ask for a new approach i ve tuned my prompts so ai will flag edge cases or blind spots i might miss i don t deeply review every line unless it s critical if the output is clean and non essential i might leave it as is but if the code is foundational i dig in for folks still learning does ai accelerate or create gaps even as an experienced dev i m always learning ai helps by letting me test ideas challenge assumptions and ask questions in real time that feedback loop is a major accelerator i m honestly surprised when people say ai hasn t helped them much i d love to see how they re using it maybe they work in very narrow domains where general purpose ai isn t as helpful but i d be really interested in seeing concrete examples so i can better understand where the friction is hope this gives some useful perspective happy to share more if anyone wants examples or follow up', 'i ve seen this topic come up a lot and people mostly seem to focus on i will lose my job have no money and live a miserable life but that is the lowest level of analysis breaking down the impact scientifically let s analyze this using economic principles technological adoption curves and market dynamics the core assumption ai can fully replace software engineers if ai is advanced enough to fully replace software engineers then building software becomes a commodity anyone can generate software instantly at little to no cost no expertise is required business executives startups and even individuals can develop software without needing developers this eliminates the traditional barrier to entry for software businesses fundamentally changing the market structure the death of traditional software companies if anyone can build an application instantly with ai why would they pay for an existing saas product software companies don t hold any unique means of production the code and knowledge were their capital and now ai can replace that entirely even ai powered saas businesses would fail because users could just instruct their own ai to create a tailored alternative analogy to industrial automation when industrial automation replaced factory workers the factories still existed and capital was still required to run production here software factories become obsolete since everyone has access to the means of production via ai the fundamental difference between software automation and previous forms of automation is that everyone can instantly produce modify and maintain software at no cost the commodification of software once software development is fully automated it follows the pattern of commoditization software loses scarcity and uniqueness as a product instead of buying software companies might rent ai computing power to generate and maintain their own software on demand or rather since those companies would be dead anyway it s actually the current end user that generates and maintain the software he generated new business models would emerge since selling prebuilt software would be meaningless businesses would shift to ai infrastructure hosting services e g renting ai computing power managed cloud solutions customization consulting helping users define and optimize their ai generated software regulation compliance ensuring ai generated software meets security legal and ethical standards however these would be significantly different from today s software businesses what happens to the economy if software is completely free then massive deflation in software costs tech companies collapse with software engineers unless they pivot to ai related services only companies with physical infrastructure data centers hardware etc maintain dominance this shift would be similar to what happened to music and movies streaming platforms like spotify and netflix devalued ownership of content ai software would devalue ownership of prebuilt apps forcing a shift to on demand generation the real winners those who own computing power ai models and infrastructure amazon aws google cloud and microsoft azure are best positioned what would happen to jobs if ai fully replaces software engineers then the need for technical expertise disappears in many fields business knowledge creativity and problem solving become more valuable non technical entrepreneurs would still not dominate software businesses as you wouldn t need to pay for them since you can generate your own product for yourself with ai however new job opportunities would emerge in ai system training oversight humans would need to guide and optimize ai ai ethics security compliance preventing biases security breaches and ensuring regulation hardware computing infrastructure since ai needs massive processing power jobs related to chip design energy management and cloud computing would increase conclusion a paradigm shift not just job loss this isn t just about losing jobs it s about the collapse of an entire economic sector and its replacement with ai driven infrastructure based business models key takeaways software becomes a commodity no company can sell software if ai can generate it instantly tech companies collapse unless they own computing power ai infrastructure or shift to new business models physical infrastructure becomes the new key asset cloud providers and chip manufacturers dominate the new economy values problem solving creativity and human oversight rather than technical implementation massive deflation in software costs shifts value from software ownership to ai as a service this is the fundamental difference between ai automation and past industrial revolutions instead of making production more efficient it eliminates the need for production entirely']

Representative_Docs: ['or try to do tic tac toe with these requirements have the user define x for an x by x board meaning support anything from x to x boards dynamically based on user input have the user define how many in a row you need to win checking for in a row on a x is very different than checking for in a row on x create a ui graphics instead of printing text out to display the board or make it web based make it player or x players have the players choose what symbol letter to use make a computer player that can play the game make easy average hard versions of the ai the mini max algorithm is commonly used make a version of the ai that uses machine learning ruin all your previous logic by supporting non perfect square boards x or x boards go crazy and remove a random square support network games non hotseat what these will teach you these will push you towards single responsibility methods and separation of concerns relying on input parameters rather than global values should stop most of your hardcoding hacks many of these will cause you to do large refactoring if not entire rewrites if you don t plan ahead for them after you are forced to rewrite for the rd time for using chatgpt procedure introduction minutes begin by introducing chatgpt and explaining what it is discuss the purpose of chatgpt and its capabilities show the class an example of a conversation with chatgpt on the smartboard instructions minutes distribute handouts that outline instructions for using chatgpt review the instructions with the class and answer any questions they may have practice minutes have students work independently or in pairs to interact with chatgpt and ask it questions encourage students to ask a variety of questions on different topics remind students to Name: 7_chatgpt_code_gpt_ask
Representative_Docs: ['so i ve occasionally looked into whether chatgpt could help me with coding not for a thesis just for fun the sorts of things it is good at are pretty standard and well structured e g i have an sql table with this structure write me a sql query that results in this kind of result or write me a function in javascript that checks if two circles are colliding or write me a function in javascript that does bicubic interpolation these are kind of classic things the sort of thing you might find on stackoverflow or elsewhere and are not very creative or are implementing some well described method in a different language or with different considerations e g you can say my data looks like this now write me a function that implements a specific method on this specific data if you got them from stackoverflow you d be expected to credit where you got them from similarly if i used something derived from chatgpt i d credit it right now if you try to use chatgpt to do more complicated things it tends to do very poorly so for example the circle collision function it writes is essentially the simplest version of collision code it will fail in many real world programming circumstances a circle with sufficient speed will warp through or inside another circle with its code which produces bizarre and erroneous results in a game setting trying to coax it to write code that didn t do that resulted in garbage it pretended to rewrite the code and add additional variables but then it never used them and just returned the same results as before all of which for me pointed to some real limits with its current iteration i could see a point being made that using chatgpt to start as a template to iterate on and learn from could be valuable quite a lot of programming works that way you find an example you see how it works you modify it to do what you need it to do whether that comes from chatgpt or stackoverflow or just googling things does not seem to change the legal ethical issues here either you acknowledge that this is done all the time or you pretend that every student is independently reinventing bubble sort either way you need to credit the source the legally problematic part of chatgpt is that its knowledge likely contains copyrighted and proprietary code that it is regurgitating to you as if it had created it this has become a problem recently apparently with amazon proprietary code has been found in chatgpt based on queries that amazon s programmers typed into it that makes the copyright status of chatgpt s output somewhat questionable', 'i m part of a team at my company that meets once a week to determine how we could use chatgpt for our companies coding purposes we also have meetings with our legal team before we submit any actual code to chatgpt just to make sure we aren t exposing anything we shouldnt now with that said we have only tested it so far with crud application code that implements complex business logic we have a meeting coming up to discuss plans on how we can test it s performance when it comes to creating structuring data pipelines in adf we plan on testing it s performance across the full stack what we determined it was good for so far it s good for refactoring code making it more readable enforcing best practices angular best practices company coding standards etc checking code logic while developing asking questions about different functions libraries we have found it s not useful for writing large blocks of complicated code with complex business logic however if you can break it down it works relatively well we created a small dummy application similar to an already existing application and asked if it could analyze our code and implement x feature gave it specifics on the feature and it failed to deliver the desired result we then broke down some of the complexity into smaller tasks such as asking it to write functions that did x and then having it rewrite that function with additional business logic or asking it to display the result of a function to the user in a specific type of drop down then having it add icons to the drop down etc it did fairly well with that now the other side to this is if you re taking the time to simplify the logic to send to chatgpt you can probably write that code relatively fast since figuring out the structure logic is generally considered the hardest part about coding to sum it up what we found so far is that chatgpt is good for refactoring checking code logic best practices asking questions about unknowns and writing small chunks of code with my current knowledge on chatgpt i d say that it s definitely not a replacement for developers but it can be a pretty useful tool just be sure to keep in mind the overall structure of your project and whether or not the solution chatgpt outputs is the solution that best fits your application', 'okay there s a lot to unpack here but i m going to answer the original question in a vacuum is it okay to use chatgpt as a way to help understand mistakes and to help explain and fix errors debugging if i don t understand something yes this is a perfectly valid use case for chatgpt however it s important to understand how it s best used i never ask chatgpt for code and i ve learned that just asking what s wrong with this code almost never works chatgpt is best when you re asking it to explain what s going on ask it to summarize a class a script explain some regex untwist some nested ternaries etc the more specific the better it is also exceptionally good for rubber ducking if you re not familiar it s when you talk about a problem out loud and in doing so come to a solution you didn t think of before since you had to explain every part of the problem to someone else in olden days talking to a rubber duck on your desk chatgpt is fantastic for this often i ll start typing out a question to chatgpt only to realize i figured out the answer as i begin to structure my question and provide context also use it to learn what you don t know asking if there are alternative ways to solve problems is great because usually you will learn a word or phrase that you can then google to read further documentation about if you do use it to write a bit of code ask it to explain what each lines does if it does something you don t understand and this is the important part take the time to understand it go outside of chatgpt and read documentation ask it to explain further etc it sounds like this is how you are using it already here are some things i ve used it for asking specific coding questions if i can t remember syntax how does javascrips array mapping work again i ll outline how i m thinking of structuring a certain part of a program and have it summarize my architecture to see if it make sense ask it to explain what some regex or a bash script is doing see if there are alternative ways of solving problems is there another way to conditionally check for this variable other than writing a bunch of if else statements etc basically the broader and more ill defined the question the less helpful your answer will be now onto the second half of your post which i think might be the bigger problem it is too mentally exhausting and disheartening hearing the amount of rudeness negativity and toxicity from people online when it comes to asking for help how are you asking these questions it is very easy to fire off a question that you may think is simple to answer but if you aren t showing that you re working to understand it you re going to be met with rudeness when you ask a question it should be specific you should provide enough broader context for people to understand the bigger picture and you should explain what you have already tried and what you are thinking of trying next that you re not sure of the latter points are important that s what tells people that you re making an effort and are really stuck just asking how can i do x doesn t tell people that you ve put in the work to read documentation try multiple solutions and are trying to help yourself you should ask something like i m looking for help with doing x for context this is part of an application that does y and z so far i ve tried a and b but that didn t work got me errors etc i was trying to do c but i found it to not be well documented and confusing would someone be able to point me in the right direction additionally the time it takes to simply help someone is similar to the time it takes to google something and it does not cost anything to help someone or be kind and understanding maybe you re asking questions the right way and just running into jerks but this statement makes me think otherwise people s time is their own and they do not owe you anything especially if you are asking for help in a public forum people don t want to help someone that hasn t indicated they re willing to help themselves and i get it when i started out four years ago i had the same attitude googling is a hard skill and asking someone who i know has the answers is so much easier but that s not how you learn you can teach yourself to fish and you re going to be such a better programmer because of it chatgpt is a great way to practice fishing for yourself practice asking it questions and take the extra second to do some research that might net you the answer before pinging someone else you ll get better at this and don t be afraid to use the tools at your disposal to get where you need to be']

Name: 8_email_emails_letter_student
Representative_Docs: ['i had a student request a letter of recommendation using ai to write his email to me it read as if he fed the ai my cv and asked it to compose an email for him the results were bizarre and my response was a polite no how can i recommend someone who cheats on a basic email task', 'usually i m suspicious of ai when it comes to emails because they re just unnecessarily long and contain a lot of flowery language that doesn t get to the point that s not always ai because its also a fairly common writing pattern in students as they start to figure out professional norms around email generally of my emails from students could be things that are a couple sentences long the exceptions being emails that need to ask questions about a technical question and provide relevant technical details an email that s paragraphs but is effectively saying something like have you updated the grade book to account for the extra credit yet is often either ai or a student who doesn t quite understand professional email norms yet worth noting that using ai in email is usually not an academic integrity issue since i m not assessing anybody s learning based on the emails they send me but it does tend to result in weirdly sounding emails that may be proposed as low effort unless you spend more time prompting then is worth it for an email', 'i had way too much time this weekend so i decided to go into one of my email inboxes and do a sort of analysis on every email sent to me by colleges a few disclaimers i have two email addresses and have in the past used both as my collegeboard act email i decided to use the one that is currently associated with my cb act accounts i believe i have never deleted a college email or unsubscribed from a college s emails though i m not completely sure about this some of the numbers may be slightly inflated due to the way i counted them described below certain colleges like xavier sent me a fuckton of emails to my other email address and sent none at all to the one i counted so this likely will not fully reflect your experience with certain colleges if you guys want i could do the same thing with my other email address if i have the time before we start here s how i counted first i would pull up all emails with that college s name in either the subject line or the sender name then i counted every email i found that was actually from that college so i wouldn t count emails i sent to them or emails from my school about them then i subtracted every email that i requested so things like questions that i asked visit reminders application updates etc wouldn t be counted finally i put that number under unsolicited emails in a spreadsheet because of the way this worked and also the fact that sometimes my email client didn t respond when i clicked the down arrow there may be a bit of deviation from the actual number but that deviation should be relatively small additionally it s not possible in my email client to filter emails by whether or not they came from a edu address so the way i ended up with my final list of what was or wasn t a college email was simply from me looking up college or university in the search bar and writing down every name i came across however this excluded a few colleges notably yale mit caltech notre dame and rice which i had to look for after the fact additionally some colleges sent me emails under different names like princeton university vs princeton office of admissions and so i may have not fully counted every single email from every single college on the list but again it would have been difficult for me to determine when that happened as a result the total number of emails i received is probably higher than just one last note who the fuck named pennsylvania s university systems there are two public university systems in pennsylvania the commonwealth system of higher education and the pennsylvania state system of higher education but for some reason penn state is in the commonwealth system and not the pennsylvania state system to make matters worse the pennsylvania state system had the brilliant idea of naming each of their campuses city university of pennsylvania and even worse some genius decided to put two of the campuses in cities named california and indiana so now you have universities with names like california university of pennsylvania and indiana university of pennsylvania which just makes it hard to search for university of california indiana university and university of pennsylvania rant anyway with all that out of the way here s what i found general stats overview colleges sent me emails overall the total number of emails i received was the number of unsolicited emails i received was out of all of these colleges the average number of unsolicited emails sent was colleges either sent me a custom application or a deadline extension i didn t apply to any of them note for the rest of this post i ll only be referring to unsolicited emails unless i specifically state otherwise top spammers the top schools that sent the most emails were louisiana state university was by far the highest at loyola marymount university at the university of iowa at case western reserve university at rensselaer polytechnic institute and tulane university at harvard college at ohio university at columbia university duquesne university and hofstra university at for columbia i included all the special programs that they sent out including their dual degree programs with trinity college dublin and sciences po if you exclude those the number of emails they sent goes down to also note that there was a three way tie for ninth so i included colleges t s the number of emails that each top university sent was princeton harvard columbia or mit uchicago yale stanford duke upenn jhu northwestern caltech dartmouth brown vanderbilt cornell rice notre dame ucla wustl as was the case in the previous list columbia sent me or depending on whether or not you count their dual degree programs interestingly harvard sent the most out of all the top s and all the ivies despite being the most well known however a large proportion of the emails they sent were specifically about financial aid and student life so i would guess that this is less of an effort to get their name out there and more to try and make it look more accessible my applications the average number of emails for colleges i applied to was only though that includes colleges that didn t send me any emails at all if you exclude colleges that didn t send any emails to me then it goes up to still lower than the average of all the colleges that sent me emails as a reminder the average number of emails sent for all colleges that sent me mail was extra things colleges sent me custom applications colleges universities sent me custom applications i limited these to those schools that send custom applications and then pretend they re something special like oh you ve been selected to apply with our special application not the ones that have custom applications open to everyone like mit or asu they were bridgewater college bridgewater choice application hampden sydney college gold crest application hofstra university select candidate application ie university apex candidate application indiana university of pennsylvania iup profile knox Name: 9_llm_llms_code_use llm
Representative_Docs: ['we have a lot in common i majored in music theory and created software to replace the mouse and keyboard with any midi instrument i teach programming one a week i think of the llm as only being able to write syntax however as far as i m concerned that was the missing piece to stress free coding it s not that writing syntax is hard it becomes muscle memory it s just bridges the gap from static analyzers debuggers and other qa tools to new code you do not need to position yourself between the qa tools and the llms stay out of that loop i m sure that based on your experience you can see how to draft two functions business logic and tests the tests verify that the intent is communicated clearly let the llm argue with your qa tools you get passing tests and perfect syntax or you exit the loop and mark the errors for human review it s really no different than running strict automated code reviews to guide human devs and assuming you ve done that you can see how humans learn to write better code in my experience hiring offshore workers for decades working with the llm really feels like working with cheap devs from the country that shall not be named in fact i was developing a system to automatically manage cheap devs right before chatgpt became popular now i send the code to the llm before asking a human to correct code so what if the llm outputs laughable code of the time when you can automatically ignore it and loop while you sleep it beats bickering with human devs about their laughable code or getting stuck on simple bugs due to lack of sleep since you need to spend x time coding', 'looking things up is fine though studies disagree with you on two points people do use it as an autocomplete and does not think about the result allt hat much and teams that relies on llms have worse code quality the only positive metric one has found with llms are developers feeling more productive that is to say it doesn t mean they are productive and again studies suggests that the overall team productivity goes down with ai usage sure you might be more efficent today but over time due to how terrible llm code is you ll be slower there s also another aspect here learning you learn by feeling out the code what works what doesn t what is cumbersome to work with what makes things easier etc using ai even though you understand what the code does by reading it you don t get that same understanding of the code if you were to type it out yourself if you re anywhere near junior level then you don t know what good code is yet you might start to get a feeling for it but that feeling goes away if you use llms as you re less encourage to actually touch the code so yeah i don t buy the efficient argument and i think usage of llms is causing blindspots in terms of recognize what bad code is as a fellow nordic i urge you to reduce the reliance on llms', 'so you re saying i should use llm but make sure to understand the given code llm provides you in order to eventually become independent from an llm and code things by my self that i know how to apply the knowledge llm taught me on different principles']

about the same issues also their usage of chegg is totally the result of my poor teaching if i was teaching well they wouldn t need to cheat i know students communicate and it s understandable how they draft the same type of complaint well i predicted that the chegg problem would be my problem but i need advice on what to do next i need to say that i am also doing tech support for the not affiliated with the department but working in the same school offered to write an email to the chair to let him know what s happening with the chegg the chair replied in a very nice way written as you would write to your best customer thank you very much for your email we are taking ai very seriously and our dean s office will contact chegg for a serious investigation he then sent a follow up Name: 12_linguistics_nlp_computational linguistics_language processing
Representative_Docs: ['as a current ma student in linguistics who has recently decided not to pursue a phd for many of the same reasons you ve listed here though i m still coming to terms to that decision because academia has been my dream for so long if linguistics is something you re passionate about pursuing there certainly are phd programs that would happily accept you even with only a minor if you have a strong and clear vision of how you could succeed in research once you arrive in the program all of the programs i ve looked at even consider students who ve never studied linguistics at all as long as you can advocate for how you have general academic skillsets and if your background is in something relevant to what you want to do research on e g people who studied comp sci in undergrad who now want to go into computational linguistics so if that s the only thing stopping you from applying for phd programs in linguistics don t let that hold you back that being said the job market in linguistics isn t any better than the job market in any other academic field and there aren t a whole lot of industry jobs in linguistics unless you go into nlp stuff the only non nlp industry job i ve ever heard of for linguistics phd holders is curriculum development at duolingo which might be cool but isn t exactly a reliable back up plan for if academia doesn t work out', 'yep agreed the linguistics focused side of cs is computational linguistics not nlp nlp seems to be more of ml applied to language', 'one of my favorite classes in college was a computational linguistics course i had an excellent professor with a background in linguistics and phd in computational linguistics that made the course fair but challenging i would suggest a possible route for you as completing your degree gt building up good foundations in computer science and programming gt apply for a graduate program in computational linguistics or computer science gt continue building up Name: 13_journal_review_reviewers_journals
Representative_Docs: ['i have just submitted a manuscript to an mdpi journal god only knows what possessed me and i am reasonably confident that all four reviews were generated by ai i was able to generate very similar reviews by uploading the manuscript to chat gpt perplexity and gemini three of the four reviews contained perfect english spelling and grammar practically unheard of with real reviews all of the reviews were exceptionally vague lacking nuance and detail when i pasted the reviewers comments into zerogpt and gptzero it indicated that the reviews were generated by ai the journal was able to produce four reviewers within three weeks of submission in my years as an academic this has never happened and finally the email from the editor suggested that i ignore any reviewers comments recommending that i include and cite articles that are unrelated to the topic of the manuscript indeed there were several such recommendations welcome to the future of peer review in an era of increasing journal publications and increased time pressure on human reviewers it is unfortunately inevitable that some journals are going to resort to review by ai', 'i work in mathematics and its applications in particular in deep learning this is the first post of a series i have in mind about the review process i advocate for open review authors know who the reviewers of their papers are and they are automatically given the whole reports and also automatically the right to reply to the objections by the reviewers why if a reviewer reveals their name then they will be hesitant to say wrong things or things they are unsure the objections by the reviewers can be right or wrong don t treat them as they are gold standards however that is far from standard in the fields i have written papers on there could be review reports like we don t think that this method has any advantage without any reason and the authors are not allowed to reply also we don t know who the reviewers are and whether they are fair or competent and often there are many cries of unfairness however there have been not much improvements i know websites like openreview which is a better model the whole public knows about the content of the review they also allow authors to reply to objections the public could more easily decide who is right and who is wrong if there is dispute so this is almost what i want except we don t know who the reviewers are but one committee which has the authority could easily find out who are behind so i have a couple of questions what is the common practice of reviewing in your field i wrote what would you like it to be at least as the openreview however i like something more than that see point below how do you think it could become what you would like it could be in my case an initial thought i have is that a concerted effort from all institutes in the whole world can resolve the problem in the field in particular there should be an independent committee having the right to audit journals and conferences and so on should there be evidences of irregularities editors reviewers who show a tendency of doing bad jobs should get consequences same for journals which have the tendency of doing bad jobs papers which were unfairly treated should be reconsidered authors with solid evidences of unfairness from the journals editors reviewers should have the right to appeal to the independent committee addendum november from the responses many agree that reviewers should be anonymous to authors i have no objections with that except how to keep reviewers to be fair and hold them in account if they too often do bad jobs for or not for their own benefits the field climate environmental science seems to have a very nice reviewing environment x b also it is suggested a website where people review the review process x b looking into some of the journals there i think that the website is very informative and useful on the other hand for each journal just about a few reviews submitted and usually not as detailed as on openreview website however how can we use the information to actually improve the reviewing process x b addendum november is there any way to design a relatively fair system of reviewing some ideas i have have a standard process that decisions must follow by specific reasons give authors the right to respond to the criticism by editors reviewers don t use the reasons too technical not up to the level instead comparing with recent papers published there probably within years depending on how many papers that journal publishes a year the important issue how to have the ideas we have actually implemented this will be the topic of another post addendum december there is a comment about journals paying fees for editors referees this could help with improving the quality of peer reviewing however there are some difficulties disadvantages first if a paper is rejected then journal has no income from that paper so they cannot pay editors referees would this make editors referees to be more willing to accept papers i don t know how to resolve this issue second some editors referees may get benefits because of getting invited to review too many papers maybe there should be a limit on how many papers an editor referee is allowed to handle a year another way is that after a certain amount of fees obtained the editor referee will do things for free like what they are doing now addendum december one idea which could alleviate the fear of referees about being known to the authors is that the journals will change the way they ask the referees to do the referees will not be asked about decision on acceptance rejection or about subjective opinions like is the paper beautiful instead the referees will be asked on a definite very objective opinions for example x b is the paper on par with at least one paper published in the journal in the last years is the paper correct is the idea new original compared to the current literature is the question to be studied in the paper discussed a lot in the past current literature need more such questions addendum december one way to make the reviewing process better is to have editors serve only a limited duration say or years for a journal for their lifetime of course there are good editors who serve whole life but it can be tempting for an editor to abuse power when staying too long in a journal in particular if that journal has big weight even if the editor in question could be a very nice person they may have some hidden bias which they don t even know which affect how they choose referees and decide whether to accept a paper x b x b x b', 'if you think the reviewer used ai then you need to raise that issue with the editor i would think an editor of a q journal would want to know if a reviewer was using ai for peer review i would at least and i would not be asking that reviewer to do anymore peer review for the journal if i found there was any veracity to those claims that s really bordering on academic misconduct and it undermines the whole point of the peer review process which has enough problems as it is so please report it to the journal']

shouldn t capitalize only the first word of everything in this section i d stick to only capitalizing game and acronyms i m also not convinced that this section is totally necessary but if you think it s useful then keep it education this section is concise and clean i like it awards and fellowships none of these are fellowships so remove that part of the title i also think you could just make these into bullets under the education section since they re all education related that part is up to you though these should be bulleted for clarity and the first one is pretty verbose so gt dean s list every semester gt awarded undergraduate research scholarship december gt certified crla level tutor april there should not be a comma between december and which you handled correctly with april technical skills move this section below your experience section only list languages you would feel comfortable answering questions about in an interview you don t have to be able to solve interview prolog for two weeks but i don t really know anything about it and i can t use it anymore be sure your list is in order of experience many recruiters assume this is the case also only list languages which you would want to work with if you really dislike a particular language for whatever reason don t go telling people i have experience with x for example i no longer list html css because i kept getting approached about web development jobs which is something i do not want to be doing you literally list every major os there s no point in that unless you have deep understanding of those oses at their cores i e unless you do actual development on the os directly i wouldn t list them on a resume also mac osx should be macos since it was renamed a year or two ago and osx was never correct it was os x since the x is a the tools section is fine i m not a big fan of these sections in general but yours isn t as boring as most people s eclipse intellij idea pycharm vim github is pretty common and really isn t useful information you could probably remove visual studio though professional experience this could be renamed experience if you like lots of extraneous whitespace here try to remove worked under that that s a preference thing write everything in the past tense or present tense and don t use passive voicing instead of analysis of talk about analyzing the resume is about you and explain what i accomplished for each project perhaps you could try something similar i d make the project description for the astronomy project shorter and then explain in more detail what you did in line description of the position and then add bullets below it like i showed above the bullets should be what you accomplished it should not be a laundry list of what you did i e don t list duties this is the single most common and egregious problem with most people s resumes in my opinion projects it s weird that you title it projects plural and then list only one surely you ve done at least one other project by renegotiating your whitespace you should have enough room to add at least one more thing here you did the laundry list thing again the game utilizes key game design concepts such game don t get me wrong it s totally fine to talk about these things but saying look i did the key game design concept thing is not helping you at all again you should make one simple line of Name: 17_layoffs_offshoring_companies_rates
Representative_Docs: ['the idea that ai is the reason for tech layoffs is more something i hear from industry outsiders not other devs but cs students professors people unaffiliated with tech etc the underlying market conditions leading to a pullback in hiring are as follows high interest rates change in tax code related to development expenses uncertainty in the economy leading to a pullback in spending historic levels of cs degree enrollment and graduation shareholders looking for cash flow over big revenue growth at any cost improvements in results from offshoring and the barrier to entry to offshoring is lower i may have missed some points anyways the only two that might have something to do with ai is improved results from offshoring everything else is more related to market forces type stuff', 'section high interest rates industry consolidation activist investors and the end of covid all had more to do with the layoffs than ai ever did', 'ai is a factor but partially it s interest rates that is the main reason the current layoffs can be explained and understood under one umbrella concept interest rates namely the concept of zirp aka zero interest rate policy after the financial crisis of central banks lowered their interest rates to near investors banks and businesses were able to borrow and lend money for little to zero interest this environment last until during trump s first term and repeated again from to due to covid so from and int r t rates were about to borrow from federal reserve this period was characterized in the tech sector as growth at all cost startups just had to prove traction and they can raise money profit can be pushed back years even decades this is no longer the case zirp ended in companies have to to prove profitability now growth at all cost is over interest rates are high and it s harder for risky ventures like tech companies and startups to raise money added to this difficulty is that ai allows a new class of startups highly technical teams with ai powered workflows that allows them to be highly profitable with lower headcount aka high rpe revenue per employee in the zirp era k rpe was good k as great now ai powered companies are surpassing million rpe investors are flocking to these companies as a result incumbents non ai native first companies are trying to adjust their ai game in order to attract investors and raise money in the meantime they re cutting headcount to save cost but also transition themselves into an rpe profile that is similar to the new class of ai startups hence the layoffs many lives will be impacted as a result higher interest rates harder to raise money need to cut down headcount to increase runway and survive at the same time ai powered startups have high rpe investors running toward them established and existing companies needs to adjust their headcount and explore ai integration in order to match rpe and demonstrate profitability if they want investors money how do you protect yourself and your career growth start using ai in your workflow so you can actually stand out ai isn t going to replace people but people who use ai will have more job security as companies are hiring newer younger talent who are used to using ai in their workflow']

about ai the field moves fast is part of the reason indeed that people focus on conferences we move fast due to the nature of the discipline not only our applied industry led research works a bit different than other applied disciplines e g pharmacology we due to the nature of the work benefit a lot from industry improvements in computational resources in fact the latest ai boom started due to the availability of cheap and powerful gpus for gp gpu computing rather than major methodological improvements due to the nature of the experiments if you are not in human robot interaction or high end robotics if someone else has the same idea as you they can beat you to it particularly in ml hence the need to claim ownership through arxiv as soo was possible moreover the structure of the papers is different as typical background related work seldom cite publications more than years ago this reduces the amount of space needed having worked with multidisciplinary coauthors i would say that journals in philosophy and psychology are actually much faster than cs they also allow for multi stage revisions something that we lack this might be due to a vicious circle people are busy reviewing for conferences journals submissions are processed slower people publish more at conferences there are of course other reasons there is a strong culture for open access which conferences tend to provide even in our journals we have been pushing for community led oa journals since the s there is also a culture for international collaborations and having in person discussions this is also why we have dagstuhl shonan aaai spring symposia etc in addition to normal conferences and workshops x b honestly i think they can fly too fast and loose it is not that straight forward as i said even within ai let alone cs as a whole there are multiple subcultures for example formal reasoning people from mas typically found at aamas ijcai kr etc tend to be overly critical of every mathematical proof and discussion while ml researchers focus more on the evaluation if you submit to a conference like neurips ijcai etc that attract submissions from multiple communities not all of your reviewers may be expert or care for the same things this is a side effect of having an ai summer and most conferences getting record number submissions every year right now given the ai summer most conferences are overrun by sota submissions and have reduced their typical reviewers to just each reviewer then has to do papers and meta reviewers way much more than that due to the sheer number and fast turnover some papers may fall from the cracks and there is an element of randomness in acceptance the biggest problem is that we do not have a second round of reviews any revisions are done on an honour system the community in theory self regulates who ignores and who doesn t feedback having said that my psychology and sociology coauthors always complain that cs reviewers are too harsh minor complains and you get a borderline score or major revisions if you ever review at cogsci you will notice this trend this is an unfortunate side effect of being used to a need get it right on the first try if you plan to publish at a top end venue', 'in most of computer science and some fields of mathematics conferences are the preferred venue of publication we have peer reviewed journals although they re typically not where cutting edge research is typically presented often times they publish extensions of work published at conferences there are certainly prestigious journals but for every journal there is a conference that is as prestigious or more prestigious to publish in if we look at google scholar s rankings we see in theory of computing the highest ranking journal comes in at with three conferences ahead of it for more examples the first journal in artificial intelligence ranks th and the first one in cryptography ranks th the difference between conferences and journals is primarily cosmetic it s just how we choose to do things on a practical level there s one major difference turn around time i can submit a paper have it rejected revise it resubmit it have it rejected revise it resubmit it and have it published all in a single calendar year that speed of that feedback cycle is far far faster than any other field i am aware of', 'where do you go to university what journals are you looking at i work in your field and i promise you re wrong about fees you should not be publishing in journals that charge you to publish they are almost exclusively predatory or journals of low repute some journals charge excess page fees but that s usually only if you re paper is longer than their desired length you should also look at conferences which in our field are peer reviewed and as important if not more than journals conferences charge attendance fees but that s very different than a publishing fee if you can t get the attendance fee covered by your university you can apply for scholarships and similar to cover the costs try looking at international journal of data science and analytics international conference on data science and advanced analytics international conference on data science and information technology acs ieee international conference on computer systems and applications those are ones that immediately come to mind that are data science specific however you should also look at publishing in conferences and journals about artificial intelligence databases and other fields related to your work if you do data science work based in another field like political science logistics urban planning etc you should publish work in journals and conferences in those fields as well']

Name: 23_citations_citation_sources_fake
Representative_Docs: ['check the citations references i just read a horrid paper a single page paragraph riddled with phony citations on the works cited page all were fake and fabricated in text citations with no corresponding citation on the works cited page that paper has earned a big ol zero and i didn t have to accuse the student of using ai she s getting nabbed for academic dishonesty so thank you ai', 'there s an empirical question here that frames any definitional ambiguity you d like to consider consider the scenarios you offer the citation is completely fake meaning it literally does not exist you cannot lose the source on something that never existed in the event that a citation is completely fake something you should be able to verify yourself as that is one of the core professional skills of all faculty in theory there are only two scenarios the student fabricated the fake citation themselves with or without the help of ai or they reproduced a fake citation from another text without verifying it in either case this is serious misconduct a citation which doesn t exist is a lie it can t be an accident there has to be intentionality you are letting yourself think that there is possible inner state of mind that might not be intention that can result in a completely invented citation and there can t be even if the student used ai and didn t check the citations it provided the student is still lying about having looked at a text they can t look at they are making a statement intended to serve either as evidence or as reference that doesn t have the support it pretends to have in scholarship that s a seriously harmful thing to do to the collective enterprise we are all engaged in but it is a bad thing outside of scholarship as well a journalist who invents non existent people for quotations or makes up a quotation that was never said is doing something destructive an analyst for a business who makes up financial data from last year is putting the business at risk etc the only way you can exempt the act of actively faking a source that does not exist from academic dishonesty is by deciding that you no longer believe in academic dishonesty as a concept that you think scholarship should be fabulistic and imaginary or that the proliferation of disinformation and lying in the public sphere means that academic dishonesty is an antiquated doctrine and we should instead be teaching students how to lie more effectively if that s where you want to go i think you probably owe it to your colleagues to articulate your convictions unless you are philosophically wedded to some kind of ultra esoteric school of thought like some kind of ultra straussianism the citation as given is fake or non existent but it is close enough to a real citation that there is a pathway to thinking the student could have garbled it or taken bad notes in this case go with your own gut if you want to believe that it was not intentional or want to think that something that might not have been intentional should be sanctioned less that s fine i would still explain to a student this citation as you gave it doesn t exist but i think you meant to cite this text which is close to your fake citation and is compatible with the nature of your citation you should be careful double check your citations because some readers both now and later in life might not interpret this error so generously by way of distinction i assign students to write a short paper on the causes of decolonization in africa one of them claims that french decolonization policy in gabon was strikingly different than elsewhere and cites mamadou pilai aubame and degaulle the brotherhood of battle and the end of empire as part of a country by country comparison the author doesn t exist the book doesn t exist and the argument it is inferred to make doesn t make sense there is no possibility of an accident here it doesn t sound like anything the student has made up something or had an ai make it up for him it doesn t matter if you are squeamish about trying to imagine the specific subjective process involved don t but the action itself is and has to be dishonest on the same assignment a student attributes aime cesaire s discourse on colonialism to frantz fanon and thinks that leopold senghor wrote cesaire s play une saison du congo but also just plain bungles the title of the journal presence africaine and thinks it was called negritudes d afrique i need to sort all this out for the student these are consequential errors but i can see how they might have happened it s not impossible that ai was involved but these are also plausibly just mistakes from carelessness and confusion', 'fake citations whether it s ai generated or not it s still an academic integrity violation to manufacture fake sources']
