Name: 25_offer_company_salary_startup
Representative_Docs: ['hello as the title suggests i recently accepted a job offer and a week later a comparable offer that i m a little bit more interested in arrived i had to accept the first offer quickly because of a legal issue concerning a m a i m going to detail the two offers and i d like to know what you d do in each situation both offers are fully remote data science jobs they re full wfh i am located in an lcol i currently have an offer from company a a unicorn that has recently been acquired the offer is k base salary k rsu annually there s also a k a year wfh stipend the company has told me that due to the m a the rsus are really worth about k i assume this is true but don t really know company b has just gotten back to me with a final offer i had to negotiate them hard and there is no room to go higher the offer is k base salary k per year performance bonus k signing bonus and k rsu annually they also said they regularly refresh rsus at annually company a is being acquired by a near household name tech firm company b is a household name tech firm neither is a faang or microsoft the rsus have the same vesting schedules i have accepted from company a before company b made their final offer and i do not feel bad about burning a bridge with a a was an exploding offer although it s entirely due to the m a and not their fault i think company b is more interesting and with the generous rsu refresher could pay more in the long term what would you do in this situation', 'hi all i recently accepted a new grad offer for a startup within sf we ll call this company a now company a has some amazing work opportunities and i d be doing a lot of machine learning to give context they re about people large and are in the health industry the offer i received is about k salary with equity after negotiation my title would be ml engineer yesterday i received an offer from another company company b people also in sf and part of fintech they have an offer of k salary with equity and a way larger current valuation my title would be software engineer company b s offer seems a lot better in terms of compensation however i think i enjoy company a s work opportunities more i also understand that this equity is basically useless unless either of these companies ipo or have a private market now coming to my question does it make sense for me to go to company a and show them this offer i received to try and get better compensated even though i ve already accepted it took a lot of negotiation from my end to even get the offer i got from company a but it still seems so far from company b s offer i don t want to upset them by negotiating after i ve signed an offer letter and then potentially lose my chance to be working with ml thanks for reading and i appreciate any input viewpoints y all may have', 'hey i am in somewhat of a good dilemma here i suppose i have some questions at the end if you guys can please help answer them sorry it s long but there s some important background info hope this can help people in similar places skip to near the end for my questions which summarize everything i m in the later stages of interviews for a few larger companies i would absolutely be about not the big tech companies but still great companies for me there s a startup that s potentially joining ycombinator this year that is still very small and i would be coming on and helping build the engineering team they already have a technical product created from high quality consultant devs at the startup i m being offered co founder status at half the base pay at the larger companies however this includes housing expenses i currently live in nyc so much of my salary here would be going toward rent and food as well as the minimum equity i do really like the product of the startup although right now it doesn t have a lot of software challenges that would appeal to me working with large infrastructure machine learning but because i am coming on in a role with a lot of ownership it s essentially cto but i downgraded it to founding engineer or something so i can stay on the technical side and avoid management i can propose and work on exciting tech features i m early in my career graduated last year and wanted to join a large structured team to get mentored and learn quickly after graduating i worked at a corporation doing boring work then left to build a mvp for another startup as a contractor i am definitely not considering any other small lt people startups besides this one because i liked the team a lot and they ve already made an offer the startup is currently self funded but they have a few options connections for securing more funds and i believe will be good enough to get into ycombinator s cohort this year their team is smart currently non technical though and has enough money to bring their product to the market in the next year or two for sure a few things that weigh heavily on my decision i m hoping to go back to school to get a cs degree second degree mostly just for the education since my first degree was a creative one and continue on to a grad school program and do research i was hoping to be able to do this next year so i would have to be working part time hours then i also really consider work life balance to be important and would rather stop working at hours a week and not take work home with me all the time which does not seem to be compatible with the vc startup life my questions are is it stupid to not join a startup as a co founder where i m taking virtually no risk drawing a decent salary after benefits are counted and not investing in any of my own money and one that has high potential to get funded or enter into ycombinator if this startup is confirmed for ycombinator what then is it just really stupid not to go to sf and live the startup life has anyone done ycombinator here before as a co founder how long did it take you to become successful or rich how much is equity really with all things considered is the time investment compatible with my future goals so if i am putting all my time with the startup and making less money for a while that leaves me less money for grad school how long will it be until i see some profits from the equity shares obviously i d see a lot of profits when there s the ipo but that must be so far down the line what about my savings throughout all this time will i be struggling to save for grad school during the early startup stage even after ycombinator s demo day am i right to prioritize a work life balance while i m relatively young as well as finding a place where i can be trained mentored by experienced software engineers are these benefits and stability of a larger company worth the trade off of equity and future profits of a startup could it be worth it to try and work out some sort of contracting consulting gig with the startup where i wouldn t be a co founder but still would have some stake in the company their team certainly wouldn t be happy if that was the way i decided to go but they could be open to it if coming with them full time was out of the question in my mind co founding a startup is not what s right for me right now if anything it d be something i d consider in years after finishing up grad school there are also a lot of side projects i want to work on both tech and art related literally in the next year which partly accounts for why i don t want to be making a startup my whole life for a while i have heard from a few people that the startup opportunity really is too good to pass up considering the team running it is able to keep it thriving through self funding alone for a while i also am sure i would be able to keep an okay work life balance hours max with the startup and return to school after a year while continuing with them part time i am not so sure about how much i can save for grad school when i m making k less than your typical sf software engineering position this is after factoring in free housing as k and free food as an additional k to the startup base salary which seems pretty generous really wrestling with myself on this decision and i would appreciate any insight or feedback that you guys can offer thanks']

Name: 27_study_flashcards_notes_app
Representative_Docs: ['link to the original article i think it is a general consensus that productivity and efficiency are perfectly achievable without the help of software and apps however why bother doing everything the analog way when there are a hundred better ways to track your progress track your streaks manage your notes schedule events track your grades and study progress sadly when we go into college university we are largely unprepared for the new type of study structure this means that we have to learn to drive the metaphorical university car on the go as it s already running otherwise we crash and burn this list of apps is in no particular order but it covers some main bases that i believe are important for young people and mainly for students who are just now learning how to build habits these include learning how to schedule email management taking notes and effective studying task management and digital housekeeping notion it would come as no surprise that my favorite productivity app would be the first one i recommend but there is a reason for that notion the self proclaimed all in one workspace is a data storage and management app that can be used to build pretty much anything i have personally used it to keep track of my finances keep track of my creative work as a freelancer manage my university assignments and so on it is free easy to use albeit it does have a rather steep learning curve in the beginning and you can use the student plan to store an unlimited amount of data on here not sure where to start with notion you can use quite a few of my personal templates through the links above forest forest is one of those apps that many people have heard of but very few have actually used it all in all the function is simple it helps you stay focused and off your phone when you start up forest s timer it plants a cute digital tree which keeps growing for the duration of the timer however there s a catch if you check your phone during this timed session the tree dies and you have to start again it s a simple trick and a bit game like so it s a fun way to stay focused todoist a strong contender to notion in my eyes todoist is a very straightforward app with a simple design and powerful functionality in its essence it allows you to just log your tasks and assign them different tags however it is much more powerful than that its strongest feat is the intuitive approach when getting input for example you just need to type p and it would assign it as priority if you type wash car today it automatically assigns the task to today it s very easy to use requires almost no technical effort and is widely used by newbies and seasoned students alike google calendar this app is already installed on most devices anyway but people tend to underuse it a lot google calendar can be much more than the place where you add your uni schedule you can add details such as zoom links and passwords share events and whole calendars with others you can have different calendars that you can hide and unhide to get a better overview of your schedule i have personally been using it to keep track of my gym sessions my lectures my assignments social activities and errands i have also managed to sync it with notion so you can use the combined functionality of both apps for maximum productivity pomodoro pomodoro is an italian word and it literally means tomato the pomodoro technique is used to refer to a type of kitchen timer in the shape of a tomato that rings after minutes it has been documented in research that a study or work schedule consisting of minutes of work then minutes of rest and repeat could lead to optimal amounts of productivity there are numerous pomodoro inspired apps out there but the one i use personally is called well pomodoro pocket at one point you will find yourself making a ton of bookmarks on your web browser and they ll keep accumulating without incentivising you to actually read any of the articles papers since we can t always read an article and make notes on the go it s important to store the link in a place where you know you ll find it later it would probably be a good idea to categorize and filter those links as well at some point all of this is achieved by the free to use app pocket google drive google drive is one of those things we all have access to but we criminally underuse it the free storage space up to gb and the syncing option across all devices make it a viable option some cases in which i have used it to store my files include needing to share a whole folder with other students needing to print something and not having a usb nearby needing a backup copy when modifying important files even if it does not directly influence your productivity the added bonus of having peace of mind with your digital files is priceless the free version may be more than enough for you and just for month you can get yourself up to gb of free space for your own needs linkedin now this may seem like a rather odd addition to a list of productivity apps linkedin is a facebook esque platform with a focus on promoting yourself and your work to employers for more and more jobs a good social presence is needed and it would largely expand the scope of your opportunities most students make the mistake of getting into linkedin a little bit too late when they are near graduation and they have missed out on a ton of internship and job opportunities in the meantime the more time you spend in the beginning getting to know the platform and posting on it the better your employment chances one day google tasks google tasks is another very useful app from the google suite that serves as a companion app to google calendar while inside the calendar app you can set events but you can also set tasks which can be ticked on and off these exist in the same workspace but can be managed separately on the tasks app if you want to set additional setting such as subtasks projects and so on it s important to know where the difference is between events and tasks here s an article to better illustrate the point evernote evernote is probably the og productivity app preceding modern ones like notion and todoist by a few years i haven t used it in quite a while but it has some perks that few other apps manage to replicate as nicely evernote is a notes management tool which has amazing ai capabilities and text recognition is its forte you can upload raw images of your notes and evernote will manage to categorise them and search up the text in them even if they are handwritten the app s interface and design look more old school so for the more hipster ones among you this app may be the perfect fit bonus traverse if there is one app that is created specifically for students and is actually proven to work by combining all the factual methods of learning it will be traverse traverse is an indie app created using the mechanism of active recall in it you can organise your notes attach your colourful images tag properly from my usage so far it resembles notion but it is hardwired to be more effective for students than for general purpose tasks its feature smart flashcards reminds you occasionally of what you need to review ensuring you are always up to date with your studies hope you found the apps helpful here are some articles to check out and potentially save to pocket window openers vs door knockers the productivity encyclopedia a guide to all things productive personal economic engine the modern way of thinking about money your employment chances one day google tasks google tasks is another very useful app from the google suite that serves as a companion app to google calendar while inside the calendar app you can set events but you can also set tasks which can be ticked on and off these exist in the same workspace but can be managed separately on the tasks app if you want to set additional setting such as subtasks projects and so on it s important to know where the difference is between events and tasks here s an article to better illustrate the point evernote evernote is probably the og productivity app preceding modern ones like notion and todoist by a few years i haven t used it in quite a while but it has some perks that few other apps manage to replicate as nicely evernote is a notes management tool which has amazing ai capabilities and text recognition is its forte you can upload raw images of your notes and evernote will manage to categorise them and search up the text in them even if they are handwritten the app s interface and design look more old school so for the more hipster ones among you this app may be the perfect fit bonus traverse if there is one app that is created specifically for students and is actually proven to work by combining all the factual methods of learning it will be traverse traverse is an indie app created using the mechanism of active recall in it you can organise your notes attach your colourful images tag properly from my usage so far it resembles notion but it is hardwired to be more effective for students than for general purpose tasks its feature smart flashcards reminds you occasionally of what you need to review ensuring you are always up to date with your studies hope you found the apps helpful here are some articles to check out and potentially save to pocket window openers vs door knockers the productivity encyclopedia a guide to all things productive personal economic engine the modern way of thinking about money peace']

Name: 29_llm_llms_using llm_use llm
Representative_Docs: ['i think you would need to specify what you are teaching e g humanities vs sciences so that we can understand where you are coming from i am in the sciences and statistics so i don t penalize the use of llm i penalize llm s bad answers of course this is more difficult in introductory courses in which llm can ace the basic questions so i need to include more difficult questions that llms get wrong i get a lot of hate for making these questions in the humanities i certainly see this as a problem if you look around there are two groups of professors of humanities those who fully embrace it and don t care that that llms are taking away the students ability to think those who ask for full documentation of how llm is used e g full disclosure of how it is used as well as before and after llm revision i am at a university where llm use is not banned it s certainly plagiarism but students who would never plagiarize do it this is exactly right submitting work that is not your own is plagiarsm it s sad that many professors don t remember this i think you ve unfairly received many downvotes probably from the arm of students who read these posts', 'there is a difference between having an llm assist with editing and tailoring the cover letter a super charged grammarly and having the llm write it from whole cloth the question to be asked is did you write the cover letter and have another entity assist with editing or did you have the other entity write the cover letter for you be it a career coach or llm the first is acceptable likewise it doesn t matter if some other human wrote it for you or if an llm wrote it the second is at issue if it is your creative thoughts that went into writing the cover letter and deciding what is in it and what is not then there shouldn t be any issue additionally the telltale fingerprints of an llm writing it won t be there the trick is to use the llm as an impartial editor rather than a generator have it tell you what types of things need to be looked at typos grammatical and punctuation errors inconsistent sentences that were left unfinished and then with its advice you edit it and repeat as necessary with that process the llm never wrote a line for you and its signature would not be in the writing', 'on reddit people always say it s impossible to detect llm written texts but in reality there is nuance while there s no absolute certainty when you submit a text which is broken down in the typical ways ai does with lots of bulletpoints and subheadings and using the same style of writing it gets pretty obvious that it s likely to have been written by an llm at my college students get failed when they submit papers which look very strongly like they are made by an llm and honestly it s understandable if you re a college professor and suddenly all your submitted papers go from the kind of crappy stuff you were used to getting to llm generated stuff which was barely even proofread by your students and you re expected to read of those word research papers to give constructive feedback only for students to put the same paper with your feedback into an llm and serve it back to you you too would get fed up with that bullshit and start failing them for it i was told i could use an llm but the main driver of the content still had to be me and if it looked too much like a straight copy paste of an llm i d get an immediate']

difficult to get in', 'hi all this is going to be a somewhat long post i am at a crossroads in my career and i highly appreciate any and all advice and pointers you can provide below i am going to describe my situation but all my thoughts are a bit hazy and so don t shy away from offering your opinions even on topics that i am not asking for advice directly my background i came to the us from another country to do my phd my phd is in mechanical engineering me in a solid but not say top university before starting my phd i did my bs and ms in aerospace engineering and worked in aerospace companies in my country for years before coming here even though my phd is in me i would say my phd research is more in ml than me i developed ml tools to solve problems in me basically i am finalizing my phd this year at the risk of sounding arrogant i will say that i am a very accomplished phd student my publication record is much higher than my peers and i have won numerous awards fellowships grants etc i mention these because later on in this thread when i say i am trying to go into a research scientist in ml position in big tech you will know that i have some things on my cv that make me believe i will or should be taken seriously as i said i had a job before coming here and i had a somewhat comfortable life the reasons that i decided to start a phd were life is much better in the us and a phd would give me a solid path to come here and i realized that my job is extremely boring and my brain was rotting away i needed to challenge myself and i found that challenge in academia after four years of working in the industry different engineering companies i fully realized that i belong in academia doing research that was until years ago around years ago i started questioning if this much commitment to get an academic position is even worth it in my field the academic market is extremely oversaturated most open faculty positions get applications with serious applications out of which are shortlisted who all have years of postdoc on top of their phd i am questioning if subjecting myself to years of postdoc with shit pay and high stress and moving from state to state for my next postdoc every year is worth it plus i have a family and i have an obligation towards my family and parents and instead of this pipe dream i should be looking for a solid job with a good pay my current situation so i started looking for jobs but jobs in me or related fields sound very boring to me i fully know that i will regret it if i go into an engineering position and once i go there it is virtually impossible to come back to academia then i will have to endure a very miserable life for the rest of my life on the other hand i really like ml i can see myself doing ml for the rest of my life if i can do research in ml and get a good pay then that is literally heaven for me because it combines my interests research and ml it solves my problems good pay and it leaves open the option to come back to academia later on even if i dislike it if i can publish papers while away i can still come back so i can t stress how much i want a research scientist position in ml the problem is i have no background or training in computer science and my ml research is mostly applied ml if that makes sense my research is ml in nature but not the kind of ml that companies need say nlp llms image processing etc rather my research involves using ml for science therefore probably it will be very hard to convince companies to take my application to these positions seriously so after all this reflection earlier this year i started applying for industry positions in ml i didn t have much of a hope when applying but i still applied for the heck of it but to my extreme surprise a faang company offered to interview me for a research scientist in ml position this was huuuge for me and filled me with self confidence i put everything aside to do some leetcode because as i said i have no cs background and these leetcode type problems are very new to me i passed the screening round and went into full loop i kept leetcoding and rigorously preparing for the ml system design and behavioral interviews i thought i had done well in all of my interviews but to my dismay they rejected my application at the end this was a huge bummer for me i was one step away from my dream job and i failed and to make it worse this company was the only company to even interview me all the other companies s of non faang companies that i applied to outright rejected me which was surprising to be honest what now i apologize for the extremely long post with my entire life story i don t even know what i am saying or what questions i am asking clearly but whatever it is providing my full background may help all i know at this point is i am in shambles maybe i am aiming too high and it was completely expected for this one chance to end up in failure but i have no idea what i am going to do now maybe i have become myopic from hoping looking at this company for so long but right now i feel like i should either become a research scientist in ml or nothing at all in my opinion the biggest obstacle in all of this is i am looking for a job in big tech where i can do research and publish which extremely limits my choices there are only companies in the world that have these kinds of posts and those companies are so prestigious that they have no shortage of proper ml researchers applying to their posts i think it is a miracle that i even got this one interview so i guess my most important question to you guys is with my background in mind what is the shortest path for me to obtain a research scientist position in ml my current plan is to start a postdoc but use my postdoc to publish a paper in neurips icml iclr or at least aim for this just to show potential recruiters that even though my phd is in me i have what it takes to do the research scientist jobs thank you for looking at my post tldr delusional me phd graduate who thinks he is worthy of a research position in pure ml with those amazing faang salaries asking how he can get to his dream']

Name: 33_calculator_calculators_math_students
Representative_Docs: ['it s best to teach students how to use it as a tool just like we did with the calculator we teach children to understand basic math before we let them use a calculator for everything students need to learn to think critically and gain background knowledge in their field before they can productively use ai within that field right now they re trying to use ai to get around the background learning and critical thinking it d be like giving kindergarteners calculators and allowing them to use the calculator in place of actually learning number sense', 'perhaps this in a way is wishful thinking but i think ai is the new calculator at some point calculators weren t always a tool that teachers let students use perhaps they still are often we would hear how we have to learn the math and how to do it because we weren t going to have a calculator in our pockets everywhere we went turns out that we do have a handy calculator everywhere we go in our pockets and sometimes on our wrist now as much as i do believe that a calculator is a good tool it is also important to teach the kids the proper way to use it and to teach them to still know how to do simple math in their head as a math teacher middle school i didn t mind calculators but we did learn how to work out problems without the calculator and later i would teach them how to use it efficiently since scientific calculators are different we would work on correct syntax for each type we had in the given classroom in my opinion it worked out well and when calculators were not appropriate for some assignments or tests the students wouldn t mind now ai may be a similar tool as teacher we can learn to navigate this new tool in order to teach our students the appropriate time to use it i do not think ai will be gone ever it already is in our pockets much sooner than the calculator ever was so we need to either work with it or be doomed by it it may be intimidating to grasp it perhaps but nothing is impossible to learn if we give it a try i m sure teachers who experienced the introduction of computers and the internet in schools had similar feelings as they weren t used to it but they go through it maybe i m just way off but i do think growth happens when you re the most out of your comfort zone so maybe ai isn t all too bad just my thoughts', 'honestly there is some merit to the idea that students should get comfortable using ai at the end of the day it is a tool that they will be using in their career but the use of ai also needs to be controlled i think about it like calculator use when students are first learning math they aren t allowed to use a calculator not because calculators are bad or wrong or even because they won t have a calculator with them all the time instead we do this so that students are able to evaluate the calculator s answer if you type into a calculator x and it spits back i think most people are going to realize that answer is wrong you don t necessarily know what the right answer is but you know that is wrong you ve done enough math without a calculator to know that x should be much bigger than and you realize that you probably hit the button instead of multiply if you do it again you will hit the right buttons and get the right answer but if you ve never done any mental math and just accept the calculator as being a god like infallible machine you will confidently spout out whatever the calculator says as fact even if there is user error or if the calculator stops working properly and starts giving out wrong answers']

Name: 38_turnitin_plagiarism_detection_turnitin ai
Representative_Docs: ['it s rough to hear how many legit papers are getting flagged by turnitin s ai detection sometimes even well written work can trigger it just because of suspiciously organized content or formatting if you re ever in doubt about turnitin s ai results and want to double check for peace of mind there s actually a community that provides turnitin plagiarism and ai checks you can upload your work there and it ll give you a clearer idea of what might get flagged without the hassle of trying to interpret false positives hope this helps anyone going through the same thing', 'what i have found is that the turnitin engine is good at red flagging assignments quickly when i then read the assignments i am able to determine quickly that yes the flagged submission is ai generated i determine this by looking for evidence of ai hallucination such as arguments that do not originate from the primary sources quotes that are not representative of anything stated by the authors and so on there is also some characteristic language in the submissions that backs up my own assessments the benefit of using turnitin s ai detection is primarily as an adjunct to your own expertise and awareness but also because students are used to getting caught by turnitin i have had a number of students argue with me in the past that their work isn t plagiarized because turnitin didn t catch it but i can clearly show them that it is a line by line paraphrase of a source it s the same with this if i see problems i respond accordingly for comparative purposes i have found that i can generate text using chatgpt that is not flagged by the other two detectors but which is flagged by turnitin however modifications to the text can make detection more difficult just as it does with plagiarism thus my position is that i am the one teaching the course not turnitin and ai detection is just one of my tools', 'the turnitin ai detector makes clear that it s essentially a beta product and cannot make any conclusions nor can any conclusions be drawn from the probability metric it presents the turnitin ai detector gives a probability of there being ai work in the paper not a match of the prose like the turnitin plagiarism detector it sounds like your professors didn t even go thru the instructor tutorial on turnitin i bet you could probably use turnitin s own statements about the tool to make your case my university has prohibited instructors from using their ai detector tool as evidence for now their plagiarism detector is pretty spectacular at matching papers to those already published online or catching students submitting each other s work from prior terms']

Name: 41_interview_questions_round_interviews
Representative_Docs: ['i haven t had my interview yet so i don t have any interview questions to share i did however have a phone interview before getting invited to the final round this consisted of talking to a principal data scientist who asked questions about my resume particularly my most recent internship what i did pressed me on my deep learning experience what conflicts i faced and how i resolved them and what my experience working in a team was like the last few minutes were allocated for me to ask questions', 'for this machine learning engineer position that i applied to the interview process has involved one screening round and two technical rounds the first round was theoretical where they asked me questions about data science ml ops system design and best practices while writing ml code i felt like i did pretty well though i had two instances where i didn t know the answer to their question amp x b the second round was a coding interview days after my dissertation submission and i was fucking burned out i even told them at the start about it just because of being open about my state of mind to which they said they were gentle on me and were going to be gentler this time they shared the question and my brain was sick of everything and just couldn t read the prompt and convert it to code i really struggled with logic and made errors in coding they didn t need the code to run they just wanted to see if i could arrive at a solution and how i approached the problem in the end i didn t even get the optimal solution that they were looking for mid interview i told them i was flustered and was really struggling with it end of that interview they said they will discuss and let hr know about the feedback this was weeks ago and i thought i was done for had moved on amp x b a few days ago i get an email saying that the feedback from tech rounds was really positive and that i am selected for the final interview which is a behavioural round about stakeholder management and showing soft skill competencies in line with the company s values now how do i go about handling the same interviewers in the final round do i say something like glad you understand i was burned out since this is gonna be a behavioural round how can i use this success through failure to my advantage am i at a disadvantage now even if i do well in the behavioural round how common is this any insights as to what could have happened amp x b tldr fucked up tech interview still selected for behavioural how do i address this when i face the same interviewers how to make the best out of it', 'hi all just got an offer for an as internship and wanted to share some details about the recruitment and interview process my background third year phd at top us university didn t need visa sponsorship research focuses on computational social science specifically automated llm annotation graph machine learning and knowledge graphs a few good pubs but in workshops and or non top tier nlp confs journals i cold applied around october in early november a recruiter reached out with oa information oa was leetcode easy and leetcode medium in about hour both didn t require any dp or crazy lc techniques just fairly simple data processing dicts two pointers etc not really anything crazy i get the sense that the questions were deliberately easier than swe intern questions oa also included a personality test component basically gave statements that you had to rate strongly agree strongly disagree i assume amazon leadership principles were important here got notified that i passed oa roughly a week after taking it recruiter sent a form to schedule two interview rounds for the loop interviews were hour long each and with people from the team i was interviewing for interviews were half leadership principle and half technical i didn t get any leetcode questions but i understand that most people do technical questions focused on transformer architecture nlp techniques and statistical inference experiment design with their business use cases questions were not from a bank but very strongly tailored to the actual intern project example questions how would you constrain the embedding space of an encoder language model what is the advantage of multihead attention how would you handle cleaning non uniformly missing data i honestly didn t do flawless on these i was especially weak on statistics because i don t work with it a ton for my research and only reviewed a bit before advice is definitely to look up the specific project and really focus your studying to things they work on leadership principle questions were pretty standard things like tell me about a time when you went beyond what was requested by a stakeholder tell me about a project that exceeded your expectations how did you handle disagreements with a supervisor etc etc you are expected to fit the leadership principles into these its generally pretty obvious which ones apply so just slightly signpost for those definitely just prepare a list of potential anecdotes from your experience and which leadership principles you demonstrated and try to fit them in they would ask questions and sometimes these ended up being technical as well like why did you select a specific model or how did you set up the pipeline implementation etc it was funny i actually told a story and the first interviewer didn t think it fit well enough so she asked me for another one especially for interns on these i think they want to help you put your best foot forward lol interviewers said i should hear back within days but i got ghosted for months i think my recruiter quit or something so i got kind of forgotten after emailing once every weeks for an update and giving up after the first few weeks my new recruiter finally emails me in late jan about how i passed the interview loop but the team went with a different candidate i was in an alternate team matching process and they would send my resume around to different hiring managers on i got an email about a potential match with some info about the project and the parts of my resume they were most interested in things went super quickly i scheduled a chat with the manager on and we met for minutes this interview was much more chill i just got to give a min pitch about how my previous experience could potentially contribute they pitched the project and then i just got to ask a few questions about their current approach how the data looks and potential deliverables evaluation on i got the offer letter so basically ish business days after the interview overall i was pretty satisfied with the process it s not insanely leetcode focused as some other mle pipelines cough cough tiktok i felt like the questions were fair and the leadership principles questions were a good way to showcase and structure experience if my recruiter didn t disappear for a few months it would ve been a very good process lol']

Name: 42_students_use ai_use_students use
Representative_Docs: ['this is so much better than blanket banning the use of ai teach your students how to use the tools available', 'the idea of trying to catch students using ai is antithetical to responsible education we are in a transition period and i do not claim to have the answers for how to move forward but one thing i am absolutely positive about is the fact that our students will use ai in the future as much as any other tool we could ever teach them maybe with the exception of reading and very basic math the only responsible approach in my humble opinion is to develop processes and systems to teach the responsible productive use of this transformative technology anything short of that is simply wasting students time i realize our education system is not set up to do that right now but the true innovators will be solving that problem not trying to catch students using the tool they will be using for the rest of their lives we will have to reinvent education to facilitate this new reality it is both terrifying and exhilarating the opportunities for students are almost infinite at this point we need to get busy retooling education to teach the responsible productive use of ai anything less is doing a disservice to our students', 'it s worth pointing out that the problem isn t ai itself it s how we choose to integrate it into the classroom right now many schools are still treating ai like some sudden uncontrollable force instead of treating it like a tool that can be managed just like calculators phones or even google itself when it first became widespread there are simple ways to reduce students misusing ai make more of the work classroom based and discussion heavy have students explain their thinking verbally or in writing require handwritten drafts or in class brainstorming before allowing typed work create assignments that ai can t easily complete personal connections classroom specific references critical thinking questions also i think it is essential that we teach students how to use ai responsibly most adults i know use it for lesson planning writing and editing emails reports resumes coding help and debugging language translating etc etc i don t think we re heading toward total brain mush dystopia i think we re facing a challenge that schools and educators can meet if we start adapting we should be teaching how to use ai as a tool it isn t going to disappear']

Name: 43_class_essay_writing_essays
Representative_Docs: ['for my writing assignments i have them do a personal reflection on some specific assigned sources they must use in text citations and the sources are usually a combination of hand selected primary and secondary sources content i ve made myself and content i ve presented in lecture then i really put a heavy emphasis on the authentic personal reflection part i tell them straight out these are not to be formal research or academic papers but more casual personal reflections about content i hate that i ve had to dumb down my writing assignments but it s the only thing i could think of as ai is not very good at sounding humanly casual personal i also added a nd rubric for each assignment called college level writing that way even if they use ai to technically meet the rest of the assignment expectations there s no way they can get a good grade because they won t meet the expectations of the second rubric in the college level writing rubric i included phrases like content ideas are present but not particularly developed or supported some evidence but usually of a broad generalized ambiguous nature content is too general and or ambiguous may read as being generated by ai run through spinbots or as a paper for purchase due to including non related content topics or sources ai is fantastic at making broad generalized statements but not very good at developing specifics or nuanced ideas no attempt at authentic personal style reads as cold overly formal bogged down in jargon or too much overly academic vernacular and or reads as non authentic non human and or there is little attempt at style reads as flat and uninteresting in content which is usually generalized and clich d and or reads as being generated by ai or run through spinbots having my assignments require a authentic personal reflection this is the one i usually use against them when it s obviously ai but i can t prove it ai tends to use overly complex jargon and it never sounds human it also helps to get a few legit writing samples from them first i usually do some extra credit writing assignments the first week of class before they know what my assignments will look like that way i can compare the writing style of the ai garbage back to the writing style they submitted that first week oh and of course what they love to do is try to use ai to just summarize content instead of personally reflect on it so in my assignments i also include this statement i do not want a regurgitation or summary of information for these assignments for these reflections i m looking for an actual thoughtful and personal reflection reflections that do not contain a personal authentic reflection that connects back to the content and reflection prompt throughout the paper and or contain too much regurgitated summary content will receive a failing grade', 'also english would it be better for the students to write multiple short essays or a couple of longer ones i do a mix with the intro courses these are usually freshmen and they have no idea what they re doing starting with a smaller paper lets them figure out how to write i give writing workshops before the paper is due but then give another one after adjusted to address whatever that specific class has produced this seems to work well i have a short compare contrast easiest structure i think and then a short research paper they then have a longer research paper followed by something short and simple as a final i used to have the longer research paper at the end but the students always rushed due to other classes and then i didn t have time to properly grade having it a few weeks earlier and then something much easier at the end works better for everyone my chair changes page requirements often but short usually means pages longer ones are anywhere from again depending on what the chair does what is the best way you have found to give students actionable feedback in a class of this size that truly helps the students build their writing skills assume that for now i don t want to consider using ai to provide student feedback i have students share their papers on google drive also through the campus for plagiarism checking this lets me see if they ve actually been working on the paper ai usage usually results in entire paragraphs if not the entire thing suddenly appearing but also means i can comment directly on the doc easier for me as well but the feedback isn t as ignored that way i don t try to have individual meetings unless requested because my class sizes are the same as yours and it s just not practical if i have to cram all of the students into a few hours i m not really interacting with them and it just means i m not spending class time going over things that would benefit them more i suppose you could require they meet with you but you d either have to set aside some class days for that or have them come to your office does splitting the class in half and assigning different due dates just cause chaos or could it help students get more individual attention i m not quite sure what that would look like do you hate yourself even if you can keep track of it you re going to have students claim they thought their due dates were later students who claim you were harsher with their group than a different group and students who say having a later earlier due date then crowded them on another assignment would writing multiple short essays and then having the students revise a couple few be the way to go i ve always paired a portfolio assignment with reflective writing done throughout the semester and would also have a reflective component for the portfolio itself some of my colleagues do this and it works for them i don t just because i give so many opportunities to get help before the due date and i give enough smaller assignments that screwing up one paper would do not nice things to the final grade but won t mean failing the class you do what works for you what else am i not considering our cap is normally and full time instructors will be teaching classes so students on average adjuncts will be teaching classes and they will be provided a syllabus to work from so they won t need to start from scratch whether or not you re new to teaching remember that it s okay to change things as you go as long as students have plenty of warning they re not really going to care if you make adjustments to improve the class', 'fight fire with fire ai assisted response to deal with all this here are some practical ways a teacher in this situation could regain meaning in their work and help students move beyond corporate smooth ai writing shift assignments toward process not just product in class writing require short written responses during class handwritten or closed device that build toward larger assignments draft checkpoints ask students to submit outlines rough drafts or annotated bibliographies before the polished essay reflections have them write a brief reflection on how they approached the assignment what choices they made and where they struggled why it helps it ties grading to authentic student work and thinking not just the final essay use oral or live components one on one conferences after submitting a paper students explain their argument or thought process in a minute chat in class presentations pair written assignments with short informal presentations voice notes or video logs students can submit a minute audio video reflection summarizing their paper why it helps it makes students accountable for what they turn in and discourages outsourcing thought redesign prompts to resist generic ai outputs personal connection ask students to relate course material to their own experiences communities or current events they care about text specificity require quotations close readings or references to class only discussions readings that ai can t easily fake creative twists mix genres write a dialogue a letter or a satirical piece based on the text why it helps generic ai writing often flattens nuance making assignments contextual and personal reduces that teach ai literacy instead of only policing compare outputs let students see how chatgpt responds to the same prompt and critique its limits what it misses what it overgeneralizes meta assignments ask students to use an ai draft but then mark up changes they made to add depth or originality genre awareness teach ai text as its own style formal padded generalized and show why real writing needs more voice and risk why it helps students learn to see ai text as a tool not a replacement for thought protect your own sanity simplify grading use checkmarks rubrics for low stakes writing so you re not investing full effort in ai heavy submissions focus energy pour more time into the assignments where you can actually see student thinking conferences in class presentations set clear policies state what ai use is acceptable if any and hold students to reflective accountability why it helps keeps grading sustainable and re centers you on meaningful teaching moments real life example one history professor i know started requiring students to keep a thinking journal handwritten minutes at the end of class when essays came in the journal showed the raw thought process students who leaned too much on ai couldn t reconcile the disconnect and grading felt less like detective work and more like rewarding genuine effort']

will also not be doing an undergraduate degree in cognitive science unless someone can convince me otherwise like psychology i think graduate programs in cog sci are flexible enough to take in a math philosophy student esp if i do undergraduate research geography just a hobby not interested in making this my career cultural anthropology just a hobby linguistics again just a hobby i m also exploring a possible budding interest in economics if i do become interested in economics then i will be much more confident in my life path a phd in economics is a really good choice from the sources i ve looked at and my school isn t too bad with respect to economics but i must first see if i care enough about economics to do a phd i know this was very long but thank you so much for reading edit just to give more information this is a list of potentially promising careers from hours notice how many of them i could go into with a math statistics degree that s one of the reasons why i care about staying in math again the reason why i m sticking so closely to hours is due to the fact that their advice is specifically tailored to people with the values ethics and life purpose that i have', 'i am thinking about philosophy especially in the analytic tradition philosophy of science is obvious i have colleagues publishing in both philosophy and science journals also logic formal epistemology philosophy of mathematics metaphysics philosophy of mind ai epistemology perception cognitive science etc some moral psychology also political philosophy could engage with some social sciences such as political science sociology etc', 'i m currently an undergraduate in my second year doing a double major in computer science and philosophy my institution university of washington is quite competitive for the former but not so much for the latter i have been very involved in computer science research and have been doing research since my first quarter in a hot field ai so i am certain that if i continued in that direction i would be able to secure a spot in a competitive cs phd program the trouble is that my heart is just tired of doing cs i love talking about the ideas but ve enjoyed the classes but it s also a very analytic department and there are no faculty who work in the philosophy of ai closest person is one person in philosophy of mind moreover philosophy of ai is a very specific and new field and the approaches are basically all analytic i don t think i m interested in philosophy of mind approaches i think they re done in a vacuum and old this is all to say that my dream of working in continental approaches to the philosophy of ai is highly improbable despite this i think that at least philosophy of ai seems to be an area of philosophy which maybe has more funding and attention due to recent events and trends than others so maybe there is some hope there i m sort of hanging on to this idea even though my department is analytic i ve done a lot of my own independent reading in continental philosophy and taken classes with continental philosophy content english comparative history of ideas comparative literature etc marxism phenomenology derrida etc so i feel like i have enough of a grasp of continental philosophy to know that i want to pursue it my parents of course want me to go to cs industry and are very unhappy about a philosophy phd my connection with them isn t too strong and ultimately if i think doing a philosophy phd is the right choice i will do it but in an ideal world i d like them to support me and to be able to support them if needed when they get older i know that given the material conditions pursuing a philosophy phd over a cs phd is pretty stupid but i really just do not want to pursue cs deep learning rigorously beyond undergrad i would love to philosophize about it from the outside where i think it needs to be what i would love for people to comment on is if i need a reality check if my goals are possible and if they are what steps i can do to maximize my possibility of actualizing it in the remaining two years of my undergrad']

Name: 52_chatbot_bot_user_api
Representative_Docs: ['chatbots are relatively new and the rise of artificial intelligence is introducing many new developments chatbots are one of the first examples where ai can be applied in practice the behavior of bots where ai is applied differs enormously from the behavior of bots where this is not applied in this article we ll tell you more about the rule based chatbot and the nlp natural language processing chatbot if you do not yet have a good idea of chatbots and conversational interfaces check out our article what is a chatbot different types of chatbots rule based bots with this type of bot communication is through pre set rules user input must conform to these pre defined rules in order to get an answer often with such bots only buttons are used since no artificial intelligence is used here an open conversation with this type of bot is not possible or very limited nlp of ai bots artificial intelligence ai is still an unclear concept for many people the idea behind this is to make robots think like humans that includes many aspects and that is why it is such a broad concept you can think of features such as logical reasoning planning and understanding languages understanding languages is especially useful when it comes to chatbots unlike the rule based bots these bots use algorithms neural networks to process natural language this is where the term nlp or natural language processing comes from the chatbot not only recognizes specific words it actually understands what someone is saying nlp chatbots learn languages in a similar way that children learn a language after having learned a number of examples they are able to make connections between questions that are asked in different ways in this way the bot understands what the question is about without being precisely programmed for it and an appropriate answer can be given in a conversation form this is also called conversational ai', 'the project me and a awesome friend is in the process of creating a bot which can reply to commonly asked questions on a social media page sorry i know it sounds a bit vague for example lets say the user of the bot s name is meh so meh creates a post on facebook not necessarily our target platform just an example now meh activates the bot and adds a link to the page that the bot should monitor now using the facebook api the bot every minutes or so grabs all the comments and reply s to the ones which it has not replayed to yet we want to integrate machine learning later on for now we are just going to use preset reply s if the bot picks up certain keywords within a question for example q can you send me a link to your discord server the bot then sees the keywords link and discord a www discord com the idea is to let the bot run on our servers and then the client can control the bot from a web page we plan on doing this in c as this is the primary language of our university and it s the one which we have the most knowledge about how we are thinking of doing the project so the plan is to create a server using a c console application which will handle the bots a website hosted on a iis server where the user can that login and configure his her bot when the user login to the website we use java script to connect to another c console server running which then is connected to a database after the user is authenticated logged in they can then start the bot after they provided a link for the bot to monitor it should be added to our bot console server to start monitoring the page for an x amount of time added our database is going to be done in microsoft sql server before i post any questions first i need to know is this a viable solution what i have tried nothing yet i just need conformation that our solution to the project is viable and if it is not for someone to maybe point me in the right direction', 'yup i ve built a few bots to be clear the youtube video you linked to isn t a telerik bot it s a bot written using the microsoft bot framework telerik has a suite of conversational ui pieces to use with bot framework personally i m a big of fan of the bot framework i ve written bots before using the slack sdk and while that s great it s very specific to slack the bot frameworks power comes from that fact that you don t write your bot against a single sdk you write it against the bot service and it translates to multiple systems so for example i wrote a bot and plugged in slack facebook and web site interfaces all driven by the same bot logic where it gets to be amazingly cool is when you hook in natural language processing nlp using something like luis i don t have much of a background in nlp but was able to set up a model to process user input for easily for example i have a bot command to find orders when i was checking for strings the user had to explicitly say order number status if any part of that was typoed or the user enterd order number status notice the spacing difference it didn t work with luis i trained a model that responds to variety of inputs event what s the status of my order the number is i didn t teach luis that exact phrases i gave it a few examples and trained the model the bot framework is awesome and there is an azure free tier for you to play around with both the bot framework and luis you get k bot messages per month and also k luis transactions per month lastly there s an emulator you can download locally to test your both you can write the bot and test it entirely locally also you can even host your bot elsewhere if you don t like azure it just takes a few things to set up but one of my bots is on digital ocean on a linux vps you do still need to use the bot service on azure for the input translation but you can have it forward to your bot running anywhere edit forgot to mention you can write the bot in c or javascript you also want to make sure you use the v sdk v is older and not as easy to use here s a tutorial']

Name: 53_writing_essay_new deal_essays
Representative_Docs: ['my elearning office had a video demonstration and discussion about the topic of ai in writing a couple of weeks ago and had an excellent discussion with some of our faculty members our extremely tentative conclusions were two fold first a short term reactive approach ai so far is not very good at scaffolding work from one assignment to another so any time you can build writing assignments that build on prior work it s more difficult to rely on ai to produce meaningful content topic proposals intro paragraphs drafts and revisions any kind of scaffolding is difficult to fake with ai and it s just generally good teaching ai so far has no concept of indexicallity it can t refer to external objects in a meaningful way if your writing prompt is based on the annotated bibliography your peers shared last week write about the trends or themes ai has absolutely no idea what to do with a prompt like that ai so far doesn t know how to synthesize knowledge or make inferences if you ask it to write a book report it can absolutely do that if you ask it to compare and contrast two different books it struggles but more interesting i think is the long term unanswered question what do writing skills even look like in a world with ubiquitous ai writing tools math curriculum used to spend time teaching students how to use a slide rule and we don t do that anymore because it s an obsolete skillset spending a ton of time teaching the mechanics and structure of writing might be headed that way as well if humans aren t needed to write mechanically correct and logical sentences then what can humans bring to the table creativity intuition persuasion analysis personal voice i would argue that if anyone is equipped to answer those questions it s writing educators', 'this is more or less an observation but for all the people claiming that ai is going to enhance student learning writing etc in my humble opinion ai won t do much for learning how to write if the ai supplies all your ideas then does the essay really matter no writing is much more than just slapping words on a page if you give a mouse a cookie in this case if you let students use the ai for some of the process they are going to take it as leeway to simulate the process and ai everything why not why not give off the illusion of process i am going to have to move everything to in person and on paper but we are also going to get a lot less done by confining some of the process to the classroom the observation everything is going to get a lot more weird as the gulf between student writing and the image they project becomes wider i didn t outright ban ai but they had to document their use of it many students chose to never consult ai those who did embrace ai were obviously the writers that struggled with the basics of writing in the first place but everyone is so hot about this topic that i thought i would let students experiment with it they had to do a lot of reflections throughout the course and the no ai camp basically wrote about themselves their thought processes their difficulties and so forth in short introspective writers who established growth and progress the pro ai camp wrote about how great ai was even when asked about their writing process they really had little to no introspection about their own abilities one of the more adept students in this pro ai camp said it s my writing but better the ai phrases ideas so much better than i ever could so that s not really her writing then right in talking with these students it became abundantly clear that they didn t care if the ai simulated life events or memories in their essays for them because they could imagine themselves in those situations they could not understand the ethics of claiming something happened when it didn t they just thought that the writing had to sound plausible for their lives anyway the gulf between written ability and perception the anti ai camp people did beautiful final presentations that were authentic natural and comprehensive the ai camp people put together horrible presentations they struggled to even talk about themselves as people even the adept ai user looked like she was going to have a breakdown as it seemed to set in that the writing she was projecting in no way matched how she presents herself tell me again how ai is not a threat to how young people learn to express themselves and ideas', 'going forward i don t know that there will be any way to determine with accuracy when a student is using ai instead of completing an assignment on their own for now ai written text is often very easy to identify it doesn t mimic mediocrity very well in my experience and the prose is fairly bland to say nothing of narrative or poetry but ai is getting better all the time and fast just a year or so ago ai generated images and vidfeo mostly looked ridiculous remember the vid of will smith eating spaghetti now you might not be able to tell the difference if you re not paying attention and more recently ai generated music has gotten much better still not great maybe but much better in the future i m thinking that teachers should assume that ai will be used and that it forms ground floor for everyone s abilities those who excel might be those who simply are best at using these tools to produce results and solve problems the best writers might still write write from scratch and then proof it with ai or they might use ai to create the base edit it for style and then use ai to proof it full disclosure my default is still to write everything myself without using ai at all but i m also not writing any articles or essays']

Name: 54_chatgpt_writing_gpt_write
Representative_Docs: ['chatgpt is useful for reflecting on what writing is and how chatgpt isn t writing in the way we think the best metaphor i ve heard is that chatgpt paints over gaps between what it has and what it s asked for with sentences and paragraphs it can slather some sentences on canvas in response to a prompt and the coat of paint can on a superficial level fill the gap in what was asked however chatgpt isn t thinking fact checking or making the sorts of considerations an actual writer would does this make sense would it fulfill the expectations of my audience no it dips into its corpus and spins out some verbiage its algorithms tell it meet the same categories as the question being asked until recently you could see these limits by telling chatgpt to do something like write unrhymed verse chatgpt would write in rhyme because in its toolset its corpus of texts its algorithms it doesn t have a tool to avoid rhyme though chatgpt can seemingly recognize rhyme if i pointed out that it has rhymed it would agree because it is filling the gap between what i m telling it with what i want to know oh yes it rhymed then it would regenerate the poem and continue to rhyme chatgpt could not consider its situation consider that it is currently rhyming and avoid the rhyme in real time it cannot think as even a poor writer could the best it can do is carry out new instructions that help it fill the superficial gap somewhat better so on my current test just now it s for for actually writing in unrhymed verse', 'it s absolutely fine most people use grammarly to improve or correct their writing unless your university has made an explicit rule against chatgpt it s fine here are chatgpt prompts that i got from twitter please revise the text to incorporate more formal vocabulary and precise terminology appropriate for an academic audience transform the sentences to eliminate colloquial language and enhance the overall scholarly tone of the document enhance the clarity and coherence of the text by implementing sophisticated sentence structures and advanced language usage rework the text to ensure a consistent application of academic conventions such as proper citation and adherence to a specific referencing style refine the text by integrating relevant scholarly sources to support and bolster the presented arguments rework the text to adhere to the conventions of scholarly objectivity refraining from personal anecdotes or emotional language elevate the language in the text by utilising appropriate synonyms and eliminating redundant or informal phrases revise the text to exhibit a more detached and objective voice characteristic of academic discourse adjust the tone of the text to be more analytical and less opinion based maintaining a focus on evidence and logical reasoning rephrase the text to maintain an appropriate level of formality while conveying complex ideas with precision transform the text to ensure a seamless flow between paragraphs and sections allowing the reader to follow the logical progression of ideas enhance the text s cohesion by implementing transitional phrases and appropriate signposts that guide the reader through the content review the text for any instances of jargon or overly technical language replacing them with explanations that aid comprehension revise the text to reflect a nuanced and balanced perspective taking into account various scholarly interpretations and viewpoints adjust the text to include more extensive critical analysis and engage with relevant counterarguments or alternative theories rework the text to adhere to the conventions of formal academic writing including proper organisation headings and subheadings transform the language in the text to convey a sense of authority and expertise showcasing a mastery of the subject matter enhance the text s academic rigour by incorporating empirical evidence data or case studies to support theoretical claims revise the text to ensure that concepts are defined clearly and concisely catering to readers who may be less familiar with the subject adjust the text to include citations not only for direct quotations but also for paraphrased ideas and borrowed concepts', 'hello there phd research fellows i have something to ask about my confusion regarding using chatgpt as a tool for my phd and other research writings so i ve been using chatgpt i know asking it to write for me entirety is not what we should do so i started using it in another way i don t ask chatgpt to write for me mainly to avoid misinformation and plagiarism as whatever it will write is going to be taken from other sources but what i have been doing is that i first write everything for example a research paper after that i go to chatgpt and give a prompt asking it to check my writing for errors of grammar and sentence structure and i also mention specifically not to add anything further to my writing only improving grammar and sentence structure this way there will be no plagiarism and misinformation in my research writing now the question is should i continue this i mean i am not asking chatgpt to write for me i am asking it to improve my writing so should i continue this']
