Name: 107_cursor_ide_vs code_code
Representative_Docs: ['i just tried out cursor an ai powered code editor and didn t expect much at first but somehow i vibe coded an entire habit tracker ios app in just days no strict plan just flow if you re into swiftui or solo building cursor ai is a crazy combo still testing it out but if you re curious or want to try the app i built let me know happy to share or answer questions also if anyone else here s using cursor how s your experience been', 'used properly cursor ai are definitely tools that would make the best engineers ie not coders even much better but there are a lot of engineers who blindly just accept what cursor ai tell them and these engineers are idiots for interviews we give a take home assignment we are fine with people using cursor chatgpt claude etc and we explicitly tell people this but when we ask you why you chose to implement something in a specific way or what other options did you consider the answer cursor did that will get you rejected on the spot', 'that s why i decided to leave feedback here hate cursor fundamental problems in my opinion based on recent posts and comments as well as experience in using cursor i would like to make a collective list of things that in my opinion steer this program on the wrong path away from usability relative to open source solutions this is my own opinion i respect others and also understand all the positive aspects of cursor the ide is not bad but many things hurt the eyes for the team these can be tips and feedback this will be a wall of text for which i apologize but it is impossible to write everything in a short sentence lack of clear communication team community there have been many times when communication with the community has failed from upcoming changes with detailed descriptions to the implementation of new models this caused among other things an avalanche of negative comments after the release of gemini lack of configurable models users get predefined models which for people without technical knowledge is practical so for developers not really there is no possibility to choose the maximum context and define your own parameters as it is for example in google ai studio for example i want to use gemini with k for one thing which should be cheaper and for thing that i want k is enough i don t need pay for m context but i have no choice application level models should be transparent looking on the site for a list of models and what context currently supports is not good such things should be built into the ide because max doesn t tell you anything during the addition of gemini max as i remember correctly me k context now has m from within the app i can t see it and not everyone wants to go to the site useless your own api in the price of i can add my api keys but composer and other key functions do not work because api can not be billed and your api can be billed if these options were locked in the free plan then i would still consider it a fairly normal move but to not be able to use your own api is overkill i can t use free at this moment gemini but i can use yours yours costs money so it costs money to use it too no logs of the number of calls to your ai model no logs of the number of calls to the tool and many other logs that would come in handy paying for each use of the api by the tool may be both a good and bad idea but asking the agent for solution x and y is like a lottery you don t know how many times the tool will be used and if it will be used for total bullshit that you would solve yourself yourself in a minute many times the model inserted outdated code in the current version of the environment and in one two lines there was an error that had to be fixed and thus i was paying extra for something i shouldn t yolo mode is disabled these errors i could solve myself because it was a name change no clear plans or subscriptions cursor costs only the trial only shows the issue of handling custom api keys and how cursor works and still many things are unknown such as the actual use of the tool with max models so far i don t understand why not introduce a more expensive plan like for uses the ability to use your own api to composer the number of uses of max models per month for sonnet and for gemini paying as much as you use would be better with version because the composer sometimes bugs sends requests and throws an error thus counting the call as if it had been made not to mention that with m gemini ciut longer call causes composer a big problem google ai works all the time so it s not the fault of the api in my opinion cursor hides too much at this point there is little transparency no modification of model parameters and payment per use at this stage has a lot of downsides if you don t know exactly what this use is being used for api keys are useless at this point unfortunately it will be interesting to see what future updates will show and if there will be a revolutionary good product out of this or if roocode and other open source will win out personally i keep my fingers crossed for each product but if i had to choose in terms of preference roocode is doing better with additional extensions and a sophisticated workflow it sometimes works like gold you just have to watch your consumption']

Name: 108_asynchronous_online_asynch_exams
Representative_Docs: ['i see a wide range of responses to the current crisis myself my preferences were to have ungraded weekly homework where the kids can look at the answers in the back of the book weekly very low stakes minute quizzes with two problems each to keep everyone abreast of expectations and then the rest of the grade comes from in person proctored exams done on paper the old fashioned way only thing i ve changed over the recent years is swapping from ungraded homework done on paper to online homework which is graded for completion at a very small fraction of the course grade it sucks because the problems don t always work well in the online setting certain skills are just not checked for like drawing graphs and of course the students rush to get the checkmark by feeding problems to wolfram symbolab photomath and even llms these days i was forced into the online hw thing early in the tenure process and i may go back to paper not sure i see a lot of people who are completely ignoring the issue and pretending like it isn t there my wife took some asynch classes from different departments in recent years some math and some physics none of them did anything to stop students from using ai on homework projects or exams beyond having some proctoring software that all the students knew how to get around ahead of time i have taken some asynch classes recently myself too most do nothing to keep students from using ai but the one i m currently enrolled in has started including bits of distractor code in the problemsets to try and catch students who are using ai i noticed one when i ran some code the other day that was copy pasted from an exercise there was a print statement that i didn t see in the original code it was typed in a tiny tiny font that nobody reading or transcribing the code would notice but when you copy paste it without formatting it s there plain as day i imagine it s for the kiddos who throw all the problems into ai and just copy paste the answers back curious how many he caught with it but the distribution i see is about of folks just swearing off all asynch which has always been my stance for the most part i don t want to teach asynch and i wouldn t trust asynch credentials to signify anything beyond copy paste skills i do take asynch classes myself because i wanna learn new shit and they can be a good way to learn they re just also really easy to abuse anyhow of the remaining who still teach at least some asnych classes way less than half are taking any proactive measures like the ai bait i mentioned earlier to catch the cheaters they might report kids when it s obvious but many don t even bother with that unless they have a slam dunk case that didn t require any effort to assemble maybe of faculty overall are doing the proactive stuff like requiring in person testing just banned at my institution for asynch requiring multiple cameras or just shifting over to oral exams good luck finding the time it s a mess', 'well it s clear that people don t want me to talk again and people criticized me for that kind of thing before but to me it s again similar to the real world not the real school as someone told me we have people working remotely i used to work while traveling lots of things we do are asynchronous being asynchronous is not new to me i was doing that many years ago there is no recipe but a lot depends to me on the asynchronous communication channels i have when i first taught asynchronous probably about years ago the asynchronous online forums were a great tool for us but of course it depends on the course people can hate me for that people may be unwilling to adapt i m not forcing anyone to do things the way i do i started teaching asynchronous classes a long time ago ai started to influence my field in the s becoming more important about years ago although in different forms so ai is not new to me either our work usually rely on ai asynchronous or not to me but asynchronous and ai have been part of my work for a long time and i think i m still doing well but that s me other people are free to ignore what i do if they are not my students', 'i used to teach online asynch and loved it for a while i was an adjunct for multiple universities while i finished my dissertation and it was honestly a great benefit to me to allow flexibility wfh and an income so i could complete my phd when covid hit during my final semester my online classes carried on with no impact while colleagues and cohort mates teaching ip scrambled after i graduated i taught a combo of asynch and zoom for another year before i got my current job where i have been teaching primarily ip but would do online asynch in summers as well as a few hybrid during the year all was well until ai i have been fortunate enough to start to pivot ip course deliverables to assignments that are pretty ai proof although for courses that require papers i do always get some clunky weird ones that i suspect are ai and grade them accordingly i also get really stilted and dumb emails from a few students all very annoying but not the absolute end of the world but online async week summer gen ed teaching with no lockdown browser or proctored tests with a limited set of types of assignments available in the lms absolutely not i have taken multiple pedagogy courses over the years and ai has made what i learned about online course design completely moot obviously some students have always either cheated in these types of courses or been so dismally engaged that they do the bare minimum to pass but usually the people who choose these courses or programs are nontraditional students who need the flexibility and they usually try hard and do reasonably well or even great consider this metaphor if you ask the average person if they would steal money even if they would never get caught they will say no that is morally wrong but if you put someone in a room with a wallet full of hundred dollar bills and no id no camera no one to know they took it what will they do maybe to raise the stakes hit them with a thousand dollar car repair just before exposing them to the wallet now what do they do not everyone will take the wallet but ai chatbots mere existence is temptation like students have never had before couple that with this cohort having formative high school years in the most stressful event in modern history and i guarantee you that people who would have never cheated before will do it online asynch may not be completely dead but you are going to be hard pressed to find as many instructors willing to do it anymore not if they have other options having a fully in person teaching load is in many ways a pain but if i can be just a modicum more certain that students have to actually master the material themselves rather than passively turn in robot generated garbage i will gladly never teach online again']

Name: 109_openai_models_revenue_billion
Representative_Docs: ['okay i read that and the premise is that openai is cheating being dishonest due to it having a part in helping to create benchmarks openai is not the only company producing models absolutely destroying these benchmarks you really think openai assisted deepseek or qwen or claude to beat benchmarks openai would literally gain massively by making sure other models score less they are financially incentivized in a large way to be sure that these other models do poorly these other models that openai has zero incentive to help do well on benchmarks are in many cases beating openai s models that suggests that the state of the art is the state of the art regardless of who is holding the torch at the moment and makes it pretty clear that the article s opinion is largely worthless if the other side of the coin is that and the best argument you can summon up against the objective quantifiable results of benchmarks then there s not much to it', 'or that openai is falling behind', 'i wasn t implying openai was a public company for private companies like openai there isn t a public p e but the principle holds for most tech businesses x earnings is what investors find reasonable assuming there s a clear path to actual profits that is why public equities all trade at these multiples if not higher even then openai is private and investors are betting on its future not the now if this is the norm for public companies why would it be any different here inference is a massive cost center for openai serving models like gpt at scale isn t cheap it is cost intensive and requires significant infrastructure investment i ve work at a company that works with openai to provide infra i now no longer work at that company and work closely with openai for model serving going forward profitability will hinge on whether they can bring inference costs down meaningfully while scaling usage idk why redditors always try to pretend gotcha']

Name: 110_chatgpt_calculator_math_gpt
Representative_Docs: ['i ve been trying to get back into shape and have been using ai paid chat gpt since my younger cousin was saying how good it is now it s good for storing data at least but some of the things it has said have not only been wrong but dangerously wrong it suggested that my mile jog was better pace than my mile when i was it suggested my daily calorie deficit was mild and at this rate i could lose a pound every other week it consistently uses average aspects for people despite the fact that i am huge this is despite the fact that i am over pounds and and it knows that the number of times i had to tell it i think it is wrong for my size is insane this could have had me in dangerous deficits and unreal expectations the one that concerned me the most was when i walked a mile walk in minutes that a kid i am coaching who did a mile run with multiple elevation changes and finished about seconds after me was only doing a light jog even after the first round of pushback it was still suggesting that if the kid was truly running that they would finish before me taking that at face value as a coach and expecting kids to do a mile run that has a steep incline faster than me doing a mile walk could have serious consequences it was implying that a fast kid who impressed me was doing a light jog and the kids who were a minute or so behind him was taking breaks it took a few pushbacks to realize how wrong that was and this was weeks ago today i saw it still sucks at math i asked it to solve x and it gave me its over usage in school is concerning but i am far more concerned about real world issues that can result from this if they don t have the knowledge to pushback especially if calculations are involved', 'additionally the calculator comparison falls flat in my opinion because using a calculator requires some basic knowledge of mathematics at the very least you need to understand how the calculator works what inputs to put in what formula to use etc in the case of word problems you need to be math literate enough to know what you re being asked to find and how to find it chat gpt takes the prompt and spits something out it s less like a calculator and more like paying someone else to do your math homework for you', 'calculators and chatgpt really aren t similar calculators give a consistently correct answer their process is instantly replicable because we know exactly how a calculator finds an answer and we can always easily do those steps ourselves that is not true for chatgpt chatgpt cannot walk us through how it creates an answer because it does not think it smashes words together in a way that mimics the practically infinite amount of information that s fed into it there s no real reasoning for what it does there s no verifiable approach or method that s part of why it isn t particularly useful as a writing tool it isn t like a calculator simply automating steps that we could otherwise do the text it produces isn t made with any sort of process truly similar to how we write and those problems are on top of the fact that chatgpt hallucinates plagiarizes and is drawn from a body of work that we simply can t access it is terrible as a research tool it is useless at giving real actionable feedback and there is no way of knowing where its words are coming from plus its prose is bad really bad']

Name: 111_lesson_lesson plan_create_plans
Representative_Docs: ['ai is a good compliment to lesson design but it won t be a supplement to actual professional lesson planning for a long time out of curiosity i had it plug out various topics into a lesson plan and they all followed the standard lesson plan formula however the topics were fairly repetitive at best almost always just a discussion but at worse the lessons were flawed in the worst example it tasked students to each have a bowling ball and a feather and to let them drop these at various heights to see them always hit the ground at the same time so it has no idea of classroom management nor the ability for students to safely drop heavy objects but also it didn t understand that the feather had air resistance preventing it from falling at the same speed as an example of effective ai use i am designing a long term colonies game where students role play as one of the original colonies and we practice going through scenarios relevant to the lesson i had the ai create unique cards to award students gold or remove gold and i had it use topics relevant to i then had it generate more cards based on actual native american tribe names this was a strong point for the ai and it allowed me to focus on the major parts of the lesson and game rather than repeating similar lines over and over', 'you can definitely re use them i certainly did typically i d tweak a new lesson plan over years before i considered it perfect often i d see problems as i taught it the first time maybe i d add a video as part of the introduction or i d remove a writing assignment that didn t add much or i d change instructions on a worksheet that had been lacking once i had it just right i d use it again and again but understand that you ll have different kids every year perhaps next year you ll end up with a more advanced class and you ll want to add an enrichment assignment at the end of that excellent lesson or you ll have a couple special ed kids and you ll need to provide modified assignments you ll always consider how next year s kids will handle that excellent lesson and you ll find new ideas maybe a new book or an idea you ll concept that you ll see somewhere and you ll want to create a new lesson plan for that at some point you may find that a lesson plan you once loved has become stale and you ll want to replace it the world will change and you ll have bend your lessons to work with what s new for example kids will use ai to turn in crappy writing they don t even understand so you might start having them do in class writing by hand and none of us know what it ll be in years you ll become a better teacher over the years and you ll want to change things but never throw away something you ve worked on something that s worked well at the least a lackluster lesson can be a good starting place for something better', 'i build websites for my classes i was told they wanted a more formal lesson plan why the website is too student facing is it missing anything nope admin just wanted to be able to skim them quickly if they chose to do so so i uploaded my site to ai with their lesson plan template and published to doc so far weeks analytics show only one document has been opened for minutes i m in an internship for admin school leadership program paid blahblahblah includes a hefty internship apart of that is being a shadow in meetings well last admin meeting was about the next faculty meeting and the new expectations for lesson plans i had some questions teachers spent roughly one hour making lesson plans were any of them looked at just the math team why because one teacher was falling short of expectations so a blanket expectation was set because of one yes but it s a good practice for all didn t it help you think through gaps in your lessons nope i just asked ai with my lesson plan district template and website to create something admin facing i shit you not the next slide for our admin meeting was my ai lesson plan as an example for the next faculty meeting awkward guess what we are still going forward with our new lesson plan expectations smh']

Name: 116_marketing_career_life_content
Representative_Docs: ['hi reddit i m a recent graduate currently about to start looking for a new job over the past few years i ve built up an interesting but rather unfocused set of experiences i ve university in with a broad based humanities degree focus in politics ir philosophy languages i am fluent in english and french and speak german decently well i secured three internships throughout the Name: 125_discussion_class_reading_students
Representative_Docs: ['i ve taught large classes too and the writing is a hard issue to deal with i ve used multiple choice and while it s easy and good questions can be written takes time and care it is also not a very good learning tool it s mostly passive assessment if you are using canvas and have a ta there are some really interesting things you could do i have found that small quizzes linked to specific concepts and as preparation for larger tests is a pretty useful model you can use mc fill in the blank and maybe even a short answer or paragraph essay type thing which your ta would correct the small quizzes are low stakes and they can all be self correcting the exams are then longer and are higher stakes i have found that mixing short and long high and low stakes benefits students you can also include not just straight questions but a video that they have to analyze or a clip of you lecturing on a topic that they have to respond to or a small passage from a reading they have to write a response to this last one isn t quite the same as a reading test you give them the passage but they have to know context and the overall concepts to be able to answer at all well so you provide an incentive to read and provide some synthetic learning and get an assessment at the same time your ta can correct those another method with canvas is discussion questions these are labor intensive but the ta can grade them you set a question the students have to respond to the question and also respond to one or two other students answers students actually tend to like this the hard part is grading the discussion responses but that s where the ta comes in and you have to provide a minimum word count or sentence count for responses to students or the students or some of them will just say that s great you could even grade them just giving credit based on meeting word count or your ta could grade content or both there s also a tool in canvas where you can set quiz questions inside a video either multiple choice or fill in the blank i think that they have to answer while watching it s been a while so i can t remember what the tool is or how to use it but a little clicking around or asking an it support person could probably solve that anyway it s a good learning and assessment tool if this is an online class you don t say you re stuck with the limitations of canvas but if it s a face to face class using canvas one thing you can do is have certain assessments online and then have essay exams in person using bluebooks or whatever no ai issues then and the ta grades the written exams there are a lot of creative things you can do to cut down on your work but actually increase student engagement', 'i teach a graduate level english literature class for esl students that is also ideally geared toward discussion and i expect students to read outside of class to avoid ai i give our reading quiz in the first minutes of class which also encourages getting there on time i call on every student to contribute to answering a more critical thinking type questions during discussion this step really shows who has or hasnt read the texts they post a pre class reaction to the text each week on our couse discussion board and they must also reply to a peers post and rather than have them summarize texts in class i check their annotations either physical or digital that they ve made while reading and twice during the semester they must formally submit annotations with evaluations of the text as well as connections to the course lecture it s a lot but each piece is relatively small and they quickly find that it is much faster for them to just do it instead of fiddling around trying to get ai to get it just right as for their term project final exam they each must choose a piece of literature we haven t read and create a presentation for the class about why i should add their story to the syllabus for next term with specific references to texts we ve read and points of discussion had in class the beginning of the semester is difficult for them as they try to get into the habit of reading and analyzing but by the time finals come around most enjoy giving their presentation and talking up their chosen text', 'i taught a seminar class a few years ago right before the pandemic that involved having in class discussions each week based on different chapters of the book they had to bring their discussion points to class showing they were prepared then broke into small groups to discuss the material together it was really great and i had a lot of fun teaching it it wasn t perfect though i knew not every student was reading their chapters and some would have bullet points entirely based on the first page of the chapter but it was still a great way to keep the class engaged throughout the semester i m trying to figure out how to have this translate into an online class though online discussion boards tend to be full of fluff and little genuine engagement i think chatgpt would struggle to write effective discussion points based on the chapters i assign but that possibility for cheating is still there it s easier for students to fudge and bs their way through pretending they knew what was in the chapters than being put on the spot to discuss the material together during class so building an entire course around having them read a book seems like a risky proposition for an online class i thought about incorporating chapter quizzes as well to verify they are reading but the teaching online classes training we ve been receiving is pretty clear on being opposed to multiple choice quizzes now for equity and academic integrity reasons any other good ideas i would really like to have them read the book as i think they will get a lot out of it and its perfect for the course but not sure if the old school seminar discussion format is suitable for an online asynchronous class']

Name: 128_homework_exams_exam_grade
Representative_Docs: ['the test questions were different from the homework no kidding can t have you memorizing the answers to the test before you ever see it but more to the point in some cases a textbook and its associated work book and quiz test booklet will be made with the following pattern in mind the homework questions are designed to be thorough if there are fifty homework questions the easy questions will often be first and the hard questions will often be last the hard questions on the homework are intended to always be harder than the questions on the tests this is because you take homework home and have plenty of time to do them whereas tests have to be done within the class period meaning you have a very limited but specific amount of time to do them test questions are designed to be easy or moderate in difficulty at most they aren t supposed to be hard because the student has a lot of test questions to answer within the given time frame so usually the test has many easy questions and a few moderately difficult questions which are all styled in a format that is is similar to the ones in the beginning and middle of the chapter homework sections not to mention the school day before the test is usually always a review day where the teacher will go over all the types of questions you need to be familiar with before taking the test if you missed review day and the teacher didn t give you access to a slideshow with the review notes and you forgot how you solved any of the dozens and dozens of homework questions that you were assigned over the past three weeks to prepare you for the test because you either didn t do them or had ai do them for you then i can understand how you might fail the test but if you actually did your homework and managed to answer the easiest questions on the homework then you should be able to get a c grade with relative ease the remaining moderately difficult questions are for the b and a grade ranges which you don t actually need to achieve to pass the class that is the way that two of my math classes worked th and th grade had decently well designed quiz test booklets that followed the above formula so that any student who actually did the homework actually read the textbook would be able to easily get a on their grade failing that class is difficult however the th grade materials i was given to teach with were not made by the same person and did not have the same design choices in mind a lot of the required homework question styles never appeared at all in the quizzes or tests i was left wondering why i was supposed to teach the students about these things if they would never use that information again even within my own math class so i get it not all educational materials are equally well made', 'long reply here i also teach in stem and right now only teach fully online classes with the ability to have a proctored final exam battling ai completely is impossible as we all know so we have started to adapt in how we structure the entire course not just individual problems or assessments the entire course is set up to prepare them to show they have learned the material on that proctored final exam and we tell them that from day one and we continually remind them of that each week they have an interactive reading assignment followed by a set of homework problems which we call practice because that s what it is practice for the quiz and final those problems can be regenerated with new random numbers as many times as they want with no penalty it s a small part of the grade and we have forums where they can post as many questions as they have when they re done with the practice they take a low stakes quiz review assignment which covers a bunch of important ideas with multiple choice questions and then a random sampling of five problems that are extremely similar to those they will see on the quiz students overwhelmingly say these are very helpful in a survey we give them in week they then move onto to the quiz it usually has or problems they must upload all work or it s a it s not proctored so at least one question on the quiz is graded by me in detail and they cannot get full credit for it unless they use the methods and notation of the course anti ai elements are often in place for that one special problem for example in the case of our statistics course they typically have to upload output from the software we use each output is unique because the numbers in the problems are randomized but i have code that gives me the answers to each question for easy grading also each of those manually graded problems is scored on a modified emrn scale that is common in spec grading systems only quiz scores over meet standards and help grow their grade and the one problem that i grade manually and which often has anti ai elements is worth so if they get no credit on that their quiz score doesn t meet standards if they do not get at least on a quiz they can spend one of a limited number of tokens we give them to re attempt the quiz but only after they do quiz corrections and reflections the new quiz has all new randomized problems with randomized numbers we are on a quarter system so there are roughly weeks to work which means we have quizzes we then give them a final exam review assignment because the final exam has questions one for each week of content the final exam review assignment has three randomized problems from each week for a total of problems the final exam then takes one problem from each group of three to get those exam questions again numbers are new and slight variations might show up compared to the quiz or review problems the final exam is in person they are allowed one page of notes our syllabus says that if they get anything below the highest grade they can earn in the course is a c again each problem is scored on that modified emrn scale we see several students who can t do squat on the final because they have been cheating all quarter and when we look at those low scores in detail it seems to match closely with those we presume have been taking short cuts all quarter again their right to fail all this has been a lot of work to set up but now that it s mostly done the process becomes mostly giving quality feedback on those quizzes using text expander helps greatly with all of this i can grade most quizzes in under three minutes but it takes more if they need quality feedback to help them learn from errors however i continue to add to my database of feedback and refine my teacher solutions to make that process faster when it comes down to it my job is now mainly to give quality feedback and to monitor progress continuously those who take learning seriously will tend to just fine in this system and so we focus on them now those cheating their way through quizzes are given the freedom to fail at most i might say something like i noticed your approach on this problem is very similar to what ai systems produce and leave it at that mostly an i see you comment no point deductions unless they use methods processes or notation that are clearly not in our reading materials overall this works good enough no system will be perfect i am just making it good enough for me and for those of my students who are there to actually engage honestly in the learning process for the rest i give them all the room they need to be the builders of their own demise grade wise', 'ah what do other professors do to assess content and what does your grading scheme look like i can share an experimental approach for one course i taught as a mathematics graduate student in the us who had to manage an undergrad course i needed to do almost everything including writing the syllabus teaching the classes setting homework and exam questions grading exams and assigning grades the only thing not done by me was grading homework which was done by undergrad graders my approach worked well at least according to the student evaluation forms a bit of background on myself first i came from hong kong i did a levels in the uk and my undergrad at university of cambridge cambridge is probably the biggest proponent of the of your grades come from exams approach with exams in may june that test everything you ve learned throughout the entire year and that exam solely determines your overall grade this approach is not due to ai cambridge has always been doing that since way before ai became a thing both hk and uk tend to be more harder exams with lower grade boundaries compared to the us cambridge mathematics third year final exam is the extreme of that with crazily positively skewed score distribution something like of the theoretical full mark will usually be enough to get the highest grade which is allocated to the top of the students and the top scorer typically get of the full mark which is probably close to what a human being can practically obtain i doubt whether the professors themselves can do much better than that back to my teaching of that one course in the us i wanted to make my students grades more heavily dependent on exams due to various reasons mostly academic integrity reasons like you mentioned but at the same time i know that american students are not used to an exam heavy approach so this is what i did in these syllabus i wrote the grade comes from exams multiple mid terms and a final homework and in class work where students solve problems in class but they can talk to each other and ask me for help however homework and in class work are mostly graded on completion instead of correctness that means anyone who handed in something will get full marks in homework and in class work even if their answers are wrong as long as they put effort in it like if i can see why a student might reasonably get it wrong or being stuck at that particular step so homework and in class work effectively become attendance grade what this effectively does is that homework and in class work grades effectively become attendance grade which everyone hopefully should get full mark in and i m very lenient on homework deadline extensions and reasons or missing classes so you need to be actually extremely lazy to not get full mark in those since everyone should get full marks in all the non exam components their grade actually solely depends on exams due to these extra free points the effective grade boundary for exams is lower which means that i need to set harder exams to make it fair fair in the sense that their final grade accurately reflect their level of understanding in course materials given my background it s quite easy for me to set such exam questions and in my opinion for a course where conceptual understanding is more important than calculation accuracy which includes this particular course harder exams is needed to distinguish how well the students understand those concepts anyway an easy exam where students can get away with memorizing calculation methods without understanding them can only test how careful they are in not making mistakes not how well they understand things the only final issue to sort out is that american students are not used the hard exam low grade boundary approach they may cry and think that they re stupid if they see a in a test even if that s supposed to be a good grade and will get them an a at the end to counter that i intentionally made it so that the exams never have full mark being or numbers that can easily be mentally converted into a scale then in every exam i give a grade for this exam which comes from estimating what grade they will end up with if they perform similarly in the remaining exams in homework and exams i put stars next to questions to indicate how difficult they are for example stars mean that it s supposed to be mentally challenging and it s fine if you can t complete it unless you re aiming for an a or a at the end all the grades my students ended up with based on their scores were reasonable students who got a or a were exactly those i think having a deep understanding of concepts during in class work sessions i keep walking around and chat with students so i have a rough idea students who got a b range grade corresponded to those i think have some partial understanding and being okay in calculations students who got a c range grade are those who seem to not understand anything but managed to memorize some calculation procedures and unreliably reproduce them none of the final grades surprised me the grade distribution looked reasonable as well by standards of my department student evaluation forms seemed to indicate that students enjoyed the course not a single negative comment was there unlike some other courses i taught lol they loved the star system of indicating question difficultly the hard exam low effective grade boundary approach made them more relaxed in the sense that they can worry less about careless mistakes and more on having a good grasp of concepts given my teaching award that year i suppose the faculty was also happy with what i did']

Name: 130_higher_higher education_education_higher ed
Representative_Docs: ['this is why ai will be the end of higher ed like it or not the public has shifted away from the idea that higher ed is about learning higher ed to the students and to the parents paying their bills is about credentialing college is required to get a job college is about figuring out what you want to do with your life college is an investment in the future pay potential none of that is about learning anything but college is expensive and if students graduate not having learned anything and still get jobs then it ll be the end corporate america could keep us on life support for a while by funding a public relations campaign echoing the virtues of education but given the political reality of this moment i don t see it happening sure every year i greet a class of freshmen that understand the basic premise that they need to learn this material to get a good grade to get a good gpa to get a degree to get a good job in their chosen career path but when things get busy or difficult they enter survival mode they don t understand that the busyness that the difficulty of the end of the semester is part of the point they don t understand that managing the multiple concurrent deadlines that doing all the work themselves and doing it well is part of the point and i actually do know that people are taking them about these things but they re teenagers of course they know better than the adults when they each inevitably get shocked by the workload and due dates piling up around thanksgiving of their first semester all that advice by the adults about learning to thrive in challenging situations gets jettisoned to bring it back to my original point ai might actually be forcibly taking us higher education back to where we once were and perhaps shouldn t have strayed from a place for scholars a place for people interested in learning for learning s sake a place anathema to the idea that higher ed should be used as job training ps i think a lot of professors in this group remember grad school very fondly because they were budding scholars surrounded by scholars and other budding scholars pretty much everyone they interacted with was trying to improve and expand upon human knowledge they went to grad school because that s what they wanted to do and conveniently forgot that most of their peers in undergrad did not have those same aspirations those students just wanted to get the degree and start their life is it any surprise that when a professor gets their first teaching job they are shocked by the lack of dedication and drive that is pretty common amongst people who just want to get the degree and start their life it took me years to learn how to let go of my selection bias and meet the students where they are and not be disappointed that they aren t as intellectually curious as i and my peers in grad school were', 'sian leah beilock across the country people are questioning the value and role of higher education and institutions particularly the elite ones are experiencing a crisis in public trust on top of that tech titans are convinced that ai will break higher education while many observers lament its corrupting influence and ask whether the mind expanding purpose and qualities of a university as one historian of education put it recently are gone forever the idea that higher education has outlived its usefulness to society however requires taking an astonishingly narrow view of the true purpose of the university higher education is not merely the transfer of knowledge we live in an age of informational opulence we are awash in readily available data but lacking discernment communication skills and empathy as a cognitive scientist i have studied the negative consequences of excessive information we are in a state of constant information overload under assault by relentless alerts updates and notifications research shows that the cognitive burden of lots of information coming at us simultaneously can negatively affect our brains and ultimately our performance especially when we are not experts in the topics we are bombarded with despite the reforms that our institutions of higher education must embark on to ensure that we are teaching our students how to think and not what to think a four year residential college experience remains one of the most powerful human environments for cultivating human qualities as dartmouth s president i see this up close our small tight knit academic community promotes interdisciplinary collaboration in ways that are both intentional and serendipitous for more than years our faculty in jewish and middle eastern studies have co taught classes and built deep trust with one another and their students it was this trust that allowed them to hold difficult sometimes painful but ultimately enlightening conversations about the heinous terrorist attacks of october and the brutal war in gaza that has followed this type of dialogue is virtually impossible to produce in online environments that are fragmented and hostile on platforms engineered to reward outrage where it is far too easy to dehumanize those with whom we disagree instead we need to create and seek out venues that are distinctly human for developing testing and debating the ideas that shape our world faculty leading small classes characterized by face to face learning and an intergenerational exchange of views are needed now more than ever the best among them show our students how to hold contradictory thoughts simultaneously how to argue the merits of viewpoints different from our own and how to make sense of a complicated world in a meaningful way something ai has yet to master students in turn take these conversations into late night debates in the dining hall or dorm room uninterrupted by the likes reposts and anonymous comments they d find online the goal of a college or university is to impart and allow the opportunity to practice the deeply human power skills critical thinking emotional intelligence ethical discernment collaborative leadership that are required to successfully and happily move into adulthood but those skills need practice and right now students are getting fewer and fewer opportunities to develop them read more', 'ai generated tldr a college professor with years of experience observes that the rapid advancement of ai is fundamentally altering the landscape of higher education traditionally college aimed to develop critical thinking and a broad knowledge base leading to wisdom and societal productivity however with the increasing ease of accessing information the role of higher education is shifting the professor questions the relevance of traditional education models such as mandatory courses outside one s major in an era where information is readily available online they argue that the concept of being educated is evolving and that higher education institutions are not adequately addressing these changes partly due to financial and structural inertia i have been teaching college for years now and been paying attention to the very quick progress of ai serving on several committees at my school dealing with instructors both trying to incorporate ai into their courses as well as those preventing students from using ai as i listen to the edu speak of administrators at my institution and other institutions they seem to be lacking the most critical concept to be considered that being the future of education especially higher ed itself it used to be that college was meant to expose you to critical thinking and also require you to learn a breadth of knowledge that knowledge facts was supposed to lead to wisdom such that you became a productive member of society at every level of education there is an assumed amount of knowledge the students have when they come in presumably having learned it from a previous class that is now turned on its head the ability to find this information will only become easier as time goes on at some point maybe in the not distant future our brains have a direct connection to the internet at what point does higher ed recognize that this completely changes our role in society i was a science major in college but was required to take a history class as part of my undergraduate education why was that presumably it was to inform me so that i was a better citizen do i remember anything that i learned not really but when i want to learn about a specific historical event now i can very easily find that information to the level that i want online i would never go back to my college history textbook how do you convince a student today that it is important for them to understand the history of europe what does it mean even to be educated in modern society i don t know the right answer to that but i can guarantee that it doesn t mean the same thing now as it did years ago and will change even more in the next years what colleges in the united states are thinking about that none that i can see likely part of the head in sand attitude is that they would have to change a lot likely reducing billing contact hours and we can t have that']

Name: 131_detectors_ai detectors_text_detection
Representative_Docs: ['exactly the ai detectors can easily label your own written text as ai generated they produce high false positive results if the text is too obviously created by an ai the editor is correct but otherwise it s just nonsense', 'the main thing is that ai detection is a statistical argument there is no way to be certain but text written purely by ai has statistical patterns that are very detectable e g overusing certain words sentence segments the longer the text the more reliable the detection can be but these arguments get muddy when human written and ai written text is mixed i e using ai to improve text without copy pasting that said many ai detectors do not seem to use statistically rigorous methods especially those that use ai themselves are at risk also some online detector saying ai is not necessarily the same as a chance of ai being used', 'copied from a comment i made elsewhere what s actually happening ai chatbots write by creating sequences of words that have high probability how does it know what sequences of words have high probability it s trained by reading every piece of text on the internet and modeling which words come after others the ai detector probably runs the text through its own ai language model and measures the probability of each word new human written text will have lower or less consistent probability because it s coming from a human ai generated text will have consistently high probability because that s what it was trained to do so the ai detector will probably trigger a false positive for any piece of text that the ai was trained on famous ones more than most because the declaration of independence is repeated and quoted all over the place it s the rare example of a human generated text that has high probability according to the ai models because those models were trained on it']

Representative_Docs: ['they might be talking about lockdown browser and monitor monitor has a lot of invasive issues that other virtual proctoring software has in that it uses ai to record a student s tools out there that don t do both of those things if not more i can understand why a student would be uncomfortable with that but at least in our institution we offer online instructors a choice to laptop this is especially true with macs and students who aren t strict about updating their os', 'as a ta and program admin at a top university i ve spent a lot of time researching and interviewing the major players in the proctoring space honorlock examity respondus and others what i ve found is that the solutions schools are paying for are not only ineffective but also a huge waste of money here s the problem current proctoring tools are riddled with gaps that students exploit and they often create more headaches for professors than they solve here s a breakdown of why current solutions fall short inability to monitor beyond the browser most proctoring tools like lockdown browsers or browser extensions only monitor activity within the browser this leaves everything else other apps desktops or external devices completely unchecked students can easily alt tab to note taking apps messaging platforms like discord or even google docs if they have multiple monitors those often go unnoticed too lack of system wide monitoring many solutions can t monitor processes running on the operating system this means students can open documents run unauthorized apps like chatgpt or virtual machines or even use remote desktop software all while the proctoring tool remains oblivious insufficient detection of external devices lockdown browsers and extensions can t detect secondary devices students often use smartphones tablets or even a second computer to cheat privacy and ethical concerns both professors and students have raised concerns about how invasive proctoring tools can be there s a lack of transparent minimally invasive solutions that balance academic integrity with privacy cheating through virtual machines students can bypass lockdown browsers by running exams in a virtual machine vm while doing other activities on the host system limited multi monitor support many tools can t effectively monitor multiple monitors or even confirm if a student has unplugged additional screens difficulty scaling for large classes current tools are often hard to scale for large classes especially when manual intervention like live proctoring is required over reliance on live proctors many solutions require live proctors which drives up costs and creates logistical headaches especially for exams in different time zones it s also invasive and awkward for students false positives and administrative overhead professors are often overwhelmed by false positives eye movements lighting changes or random sounds flagged as suspicious complicated exam setup many proctoring solutions have onboarding processes that take months that s ridiculous it should take minutes to set up an exam no solution for admissions interviews during admissions interviews cheating is rampant scripted answers searching for answers mid interview and more yet there s no tool to address this after getting frustrated that none of the proctoring tools were effective and or really fit a modern Name: 134_exponential_moore law_moore_exponential growth
Representative_Docs: ['years ago no one even has heard of ai today they are driving cars predicting illnesses processing images and tracking people i really don t see why people are so pessimistic about the future of ai i really think in years given the current rate of improvement almost anything imaginable and unimaginable can be done idk i guess i m a little too optimistic about the future of tech and human innovation regular people who s not in tech', 'years ago no one even has heard of ai today they are driving cars predicting illnesses processing images and tracking people i really don t see why people are so pessimistic about the future of ai i really think in years given the current rate of improvement almost anything imaginable and unimaginable can be done idk i guess i m a little too optimistic about the future of tech and human innovation regular people who s not in tech', 'there s no actual law that guarantees perpetual exponential growth for anything and moore s law was more held as an observation we live in a world that ultimately has finite resources and constraints and most observations of exponential growth is really logistic growth where growth ultimately slows down once an inflection point is reached you see this in ecology with animal populations and even in semiconductors the rate of performance improvements for cpu single threaded performance is a far cry compared to decades ago transformer based llms are having a good run and certainly exceeded most people s expectations of them in terms of capability but there are broader signs of slowdowns and it s unclear what the ceiling for them will end up being so much of the open internet was already scraped for training data and getting additional high quality data is going to get harder and harder especially with the internet already being polluted with ai generated content future innovations may start a new exponential growth curve and improve long term planning and adaption tasks that current models struggle with but those aren t guaranteed the history of ai is full of hype cycles and ai winters when the hype didn t pan out and i m not convinced that we hit the final path to superhuman ai']

Name: 135_llms_llm_llms ai_trained
Representative_Docs: ['i just hate how llms have co opted the term ai ai in cs is a general field with a large set of algorithms all intended for different problems llms is a small subset of machine learning which is a subset of ai techniques more importantly llms are not the general ai that people think it is nor is it a truth seeking device thus replacing your own critical thinking with something that in essence regurgitates memorized information without the reasoning or ability to make abstract connections to solve novel problems is lazy and literally brain rot it is nothing more than a statistical sentence generator an advanced chinese room program still as educators and academia being the place for open discussion we should know that this tool exists and should talk to our students about its use case and why it should not be the replacement for ones own critical thinking importantly if they use llms to generate solutions that cause problems later on that they will still be on the hook for such issues as they essentially approved the solutions by putting them in place even if those solutions end up harming others', 'i ll remind everyone that llms are not ai we do not have ai yet', 'this isn t entirely accurate modern and powerful llms can be capable of telling you when they don t know something but they have to be specifically trained to do it and to recognise when there is a sufficient lack of confidence a problem can arise in that training the llm to recognise its lack of knowledge by which we mean to recognise that it s unable to answer a question with a high probability reasonable answer tends to be rather context dependent so even modern llms trained to recognise this often fail at it if faced with something other than a simple question how this works in practice is you probe the llms understanding of certain topics to discover where it has gaps in its knowledge training you then fine tune it to respond with i don t know when asked questions on those gaps it then is able to extrapolate to some degree to other areas where it has gaps in its knowledge where its answers are based on chains of low probability responses it s not a massive technological leap it s an adaptation of the llm s limitations and ways of processing data llms don t think like people do but the idea that understanding the relationship between words doesn t carry with it the ability to simulate knowledge and even reasoning under some circumstances doesn t hold water']

Name: 139_cloud_aws_google cloud_certification
Representative_Docs: ['pick a platform google cloud platform amazon web services microsoft azure there are others but these are the top that companies are using it s subjective but i m going to say gcp is the most interesting and enjoyable to work with a lot of companies are using azure and aws to run their enterprise stuff but are moving towards gcp for their machine learning ai and analytics then complete the entry level certification for your chosen platform e g google cloud associate cloud engineer some say certifications are useless but it s almost necessary now to get a recruiters attention gt worth mentioning aws is the most popular platform used followed by azure then gcp spend time just being hands on and experimenting the three platforms have a fairly reasonable free tier so it shouldn t cost you a thing to spin up some vms deploy some websites play around with networks and firewalls remember to clean up to avoid surprise bills acloudguru is a good site to do some learning and understand whats on the certification exams and they also provide you with a sandbox playground environment when doing labs if you do happen to go the gcp route qwiklabs by google is a great site to get hands on practical experience building real world things in safe sandbox environments too i m a fan of gcp if you can t tell but you can t go wrong with any of those three', 'aws cloud certifications or any of the other cloud offerings google cloud azure aws is the biggest though the exams cost around i believe but a udemy course in the relevant field is enough prep there are associate levels to choose from developer solution architect sysops and then a bunch of specialties beyond that machine learning security advanced networking etc and a couple of professional exams at the top architecture and devops', 'prerequisites google cloud certifications are one of the most sought after in the it industry as they validate the holder s ability to use google cloud technology the certification opens up a lot of opportunities for the holder and increases their chances of getting hired to high paying jobs what is gcp certification and what does it entail to understand the gcp certificate let s first look at the google cloud platform google cloud platform is an infrastructure as a service iaas platform that offers a varied collection of services and products that businesses use to improve the products and services they offer to clients and end users the google cloud platform gcp certifications are role based and evaluate your ability to use google cloud products to carry out specific activities to obtain the distinct certifications you must pass google s assessment exams which test your knowledge skills and expertise in a range of cloud fields why should you get gcp certification cloud computing is one of the fastest growing industries a google cloud platform certification will help improve your skills and demonstrate your expertise and commitment to your area here are some stats that highlight the importance of gcp certification cloud based positions are among the top ten most in demand it careers in the average cloud remuneration is more than the global average it salary the google cloud professional data engineer certification was one of the top highest paying qualifications in how long does it take to obtain gcp certification when you register for the exams your login details will be sent to you via mail so you can take it at the appropriate time the amount of time the exam takes is dependent on the individual especially the materials they studied if you take a google cloud certification course before the exams you ll be better prepared and spend less time tackling the questions once you re done with the exams it will take to business days for you to receive the results types of google cloud certifications google currently offers cloud certifications categorized into foundational associate professional and looker google cloud foundational level certification the foundational level certification validates a broad understanding of cloud concepts as well as google cloud products services tools features benefits and use cases with an emphasis on google cloud capabilities google cloud digital leader exam this certification is intended for non technical individuals who may provide value to their organization by learning about the cloud but have little or no hands on experience with google cloud prerequisite there s no hand on experience requirement for this certification you just need a general understanding of how the cloud works google cloud associate level certifications the associate level certification tests applicants on the basic skills of creating monitoring and maintaining google cloud projects this certification is intended for those who want to operate daily in a cloud environment it s an excellent starting point for people new to cloud computing and can serve as a stepping stone to higher level qualifications associate cloud engineer google cloud certified this certification exam covers the fundamentals of installing maintaining and observing projects on google cloud it assesses your ability to do the following tasks create a solution environment that is cloud based create and setup a cloud solution install and set up a cloud solution ensure the smooth operation of a cloud solution set up security and access prerequisite it is recommended to have at least up to months of hands on experience using google cloud before taking this associate cloud engineer google cloud certification exam google cloud professional certifications google cloud professional certificates are awarded to those who have worked in the industry and are familiar with google cloud products the exam assesses advanced skills in areas spanning from design to implementation and management as well as key technical work activities it s suitable for individuals with industry knowledge and experience with google cloud products and solutions prerequisite it s recommended that you have at least three years of industry experience including over a year of google cloud experience to take this exam there are now eight professional certifications available the cloud architect professional certification a cloud architect is in charge of translating the technical requirements of a project into the architecture and design that will drive the outcome the professional google cloud platform architect exam assesses your knowledge of designing building and managing google cloud platforms cloud developer professional certification a cloud developer employs google recommended methodologies and technology to build scalable and highly available apps that leverage fully managed services the exams assess your ability to develop deploy and manage cloud native apps that are highly scalable available and dependable data engineer professional certification a data engineer gathers transforms and publishes data to allow for data driven decision making they are in charge of the planning design and implementation of data processing systems the exam tests your abilities to develop and manage data processing systems and machine learning models cloud devops engineer gcp devops engineers are responsible for making development processes as efficient as possible while preserving service dependability and delivery speed the professional devops engineer for google cloud platform exam assesses your abilities to apply site reliability engineering concepts to a service enhance the effectiveness of your service implement service monitoring strategies and more cloud security engineer cloud security engineers help organizations develop and implement secure cloud infrastructures using security best practices and industry requirements the professional google cloud security engineer exam assesses the ability to configure access and network security to a cloud solution ensure data protection compliance and more collaboration engineer this certification is intended for people who wish to rise to the level of g suite specialist in the future the engineer exam assesses your ability to plan and execute access and authorization to google workspace manage the user resource and shared drive lifecycles control and configure google workspace and more candidates should have at least one year of experience with g suite administration and at least three years of industry experience cloud networks engineer google cloud network engineers implement and manage network designs such as vpcs hybrid connectivity and network services the exam tests your ability to create and prototype a network configure a gcp vpc configure the network services and more google cloud machine learning engineer a machine learning engineer develops and enhances machine learning models to solve business challenges the professional google cloud platform machine learning engineer exam assesses your ability to frame ml problems architect ml solutions develop ml models automate orchestrate ml pipelines and more looker certification looker allows you to perform in depth data analysis aggregate insights from several data sources construct effective data driven workflows and build custom data apps look ml developer the exam will test your model management skills including your ability to diagnose current model issues apply data security requirements generate lookml objects and manage the health of lookml projects prerequisite at least months looker experience gcp certification fees gcp certification costs between for the foundational level and for the professional level whether you re seeking your professional development or being sponsored by your employer this is a low cost choice how to prepare for the google cloud certification exam leverage google provided resources each certification has its own learning path through which you can access gcp s documentation and other resources to prepare for the exams also consider registering a free gcp account you ll need a lot of experience with the platform to pass the exams a Name: 146_web_dev_development_th
Representative_Docs: ['i already made a similar post sharing an article i wrote about my experience unfortunately it has been taken down automatically due to too many reports i think it might have been due to the article being behind medium s paywall since i think i received a great response for it and i think it s a pity it s not available anymore on reddit i want to share my favorite tips from the article hoping it can help my fellow redditors and to say thanks to theis community that helped me so much in my journey my background is in chemical engineering dropped out the final academic year due to not liking the major i worked for one year in a technology company in an unrelated position when i decided that i wanted to learn programming and become a developer i did not want to start university again due to bad experiences or join a bootcamp and spend thousands without being sure that it was the right path for me so i moved back with my parents and started studying like i never did before in my life when you are passionate about a subject the hours just fly by after one year of dedication missed opportunities due to covid and failed interviews i finally aced the interview for a fantastic company that saw my dedication and passion harvard s introduction to computer science cs is just great if you started recently or you are intermediate you should definitely take this course it teaches fundamentals that too often get ignored by self taught students and it puts the emphasis on being independent and learning to do it on your own it s definitely the thing that put me on the right track after months of not following a rigorous roadmap and not learning as much as i could the staff is phenomenal and there is a free certificate if you complete every assignment and a final project of your choice projects are vital this is relevant for everyone don t just stick to online courses or your lectures reading about programming without actually doing it is as likely of making you a programmer as reading cookbooks without cooking is of making you a chef the best way to avoid tutorial hell and actually become competent is putting everything you learn to good use with projects based on the material you are currently studying they are also great to showcase on your resume if you have don t have much relevant experience but you have to be the one to bring them up because in my experience recruiters don t really have the time to check your github repo commit to something another way to get stuck without making progress is trying to learn too many things at the same time without having a focus or changing subject too often learning the right thing is not as important as actually having a deep understanding of any topic after you learn the first language or framework the second is drastically easier that shouldn t stop you from trying different things i was sure artificial intelligence would be my passion when i realized that training models is actually quite boring and sometimes frustrating just be sure that you dedicate enough time to each endeavor don t wait too much before applying you will never feel ready for your first job because you probably aren t most companies at least the good ones hire juniors knowing well that they will lose them money for months before they are competent enough to bring profit they are investing in you and committing to your improvement instead of waiting more ask a friend who is in the field or even people you don t know on social media but are willing to help if your portfolio and knowledge are at the level necessary to pass a technical interview networking is key if you don t have a brilliant resume let s be honest the job market is absolutely screwed every posting on linkedin has hundreds of candidates some of them seniors or mid level applying for lower level positions unless you just graduated from cornell or mti you will have to send hundreds of applications before receiving any answer that s why networking is so important use your social media like reddit twitter and linkedin actively participate in job fairs and conventions physical or virtual try meeting people that work in the sector you aspire to be in and ask them for advice even a cold message on linkedin can do wonders if you keep the interaction polite i got at least a couple of interviews from messaging recruiters on linkedin and i actually found my current company because the founder saw my profile on angellist and liked it enough to message and ask me to send my resume the fact that we had a personal interaction really helped me in passing the first selection stage prepare for the interview like it or not algorithms data structures and leetcode are the standard for many technical interviews you can definitely find jobs that ask domain specific questions but you can hardly afford to shrink your already small opportunities pool don t forget that your interviewers are people being able to communicate how you reason is more important than arriving immediately at the right solution also be likable and confident in behavioral interviews and do your research companies want people that are committed to working with them learn from your rejections you will get rejected it s part of life i failed really important interviews for positions that i cared a lot about but every time i took it as a lesson to improve on my weaknesses but also don t take it too harshly being deemed not ready for an opportunity doesn t mean you aren t good enough but that you have to keep looking for the right place and the right people that will be able to see your potential good luck to everyone i hope you will be able to achieve your dream like i was able to', 'not a question but wanted to bring some good news to the sub i just finished my first week as a junior developer for a fintech company and it was great my background is in chemical engineering dropped out the final academic year due to not liking the major i
