Name: 187_matlab_toolbox_python_simulink
Representative_Docs: ['hi in some point i would agree with u kahvind i love python and after a year using matlab in master s control systems i decieded to swtich to python but my experience python vs matlab a lot of my classmates did not made the same decition python is slower and i mean really slower compared to matlab and especially in it s matrix calculations which you can be using a lot in mechanism simulations or some machine learning algorithms those are the two field i am familiar with using python and matlab why not matlab and possible alternative in my surrounding mechanical engineering in middle europe is matlab hated mainly because of it s price and closed community what was exactly the reason i decided to go with python but as an more similar alternative to matlab there is julia for instance i don t know much about this language but one of my classmates sees a big perspective in it and considers it as language that is going to replace matlab advice that i found when i was looking for language to stick with i believe that the best way to decide which language to go with is what you want to do with it there are different right tools for different kind of tasks i decieded what tasks i want to solve in the future and then found the right tool for it i hope it helps at least a bit', 'python is used a lot in industry both due to matlab s extremely high licensing costs licensing issues deployment complications etc it isn t used universally in industry though since matlab has some benefits in particular fields it really depends on what you do so knowing both will be a big benefit they can do a lot of the same things and have roughly comparable performance on average but each is slightly better at certain things matlab is better at pure linear algebra simple graphing calculator type stuff if it happens to have a function that does exactly what you want that python lacks or if you need simulink python is better if your program is more than a couple of functions if your dataset is bigger more complicated messier or higher dimensional if you want to do machine learning or computer vision if you need to interact with other hardware software or web resources of you need to make your own data structures or if you need more interactive plots more complicated plots fine control of your plots or more unusual plots types in personally use python whenever i have a choice since in most real world situations where things get remotely messy or complicated python tends to win out in fields i work in one big difference is philosophical and may take some getting used to python is designed to encourage good programming practices this means in practice you need to be a bit more explicit about what you want to do python is less likely to try to guess when something is ambiguous is less likely to mislead you about what is going on and overall it is generally clearer what a python program wi do than a corresponding matlab program due to the much fewer corner cases and less inconsistent behavior a big problem i see is trying to write matlab code in python you can do it but it will be longer uglier and slower than if you took advantage of what python offers a lot of annoyance i see from matlab users trying to switch to python is from them trying to force it to do things the way matlab does them when really the matlab way of doing things is often just a work around for flaws in the matlab language and python offers much better ways to do it so you can jump into python and start programming useable programs in a few hours but it will likely take longer to learn how to actually let python help you', 'gt how different are the two for the languages themselves python can do essentially everything matlab can the matlab language is better at matrix math while python language is better at pretty much anything else such as array math complicated data sets etc python however can do a lot of things that are hard or nearly impossible to do in matlab that being said there is a lot more to matlab and python than just the languages themselves both matlab and python have a bunch of specific tools built on top of the language matlab has a bunch of such tools that python lacks or are much weaker in python and python has a bunch of tools that matlab lacks or are much weaker in matlab which language s tools are better suited to your needs depends on the specifics of the project so you first need to figure out what exactly you want to do then check which has the tools you need that being said matlab can be very expensive if you aren t a student and even if you are a student the student version is likely missing the particular tools you would need for this project so it may simply not be an option practically speaking python on the other hand is free that doesn t mean it is bad there are a lot of massive companies involved in supporting it because they use it for their own purposes that being said matlab comes bundled with another completely different programming languages called simulink which can be helpful for some although not all simulation tasks although there are some free programming languages filling the same role as simulink that can be used with python they are not on the same level as simulink unfortunately i don t know that much about simulink so i can t advise you there gt what are the primary uses for python matlab matlab was designed to make it easier to do matrix math and plotting of fortran arrays hence the name which is short for matrix laboratory the entire language is built around that it has added on a lot of additional features although not in a very cohesive or well planned way in my opinion but its strong suit remains centered around matrix math signal processing number crunching of simple data sets etc it has a lot of very powerful tools centered around these tasks it is used in most areas of science and engineering although i feel it is used for a lot of things it isn t very well suited for such as gui design and complex data sets python was originally invented to act as a glue language for controlling lower levels tools in your computer however it has been designed all along as a general purpose language with a focus on producing clear easy to write and easy to read code all components go through a careful vetting process that has resulted in one of the more cohesive and easy to understand languages although python is still heavily used in controlling lower level tasks in your computer it is now used for a wide variety of other completely unrelated tasks it is the primary competitor of matlab for scientific and engineering software development it is has recently edged out r a statistics oriented programming language as the primary language for machine learning and big data it is one of the main languages used for handling web pages it is used for creating guis and it is used within other programs such as d modeling programs to let the user implement scripts for the program it may seem a bit strange to have such wildly different groups using the same language but it has proven beneficial for example the website handling tools have been used to allow python numeric programs to easily download datasets off websites and to create browser based data analysis tools']

Name: 188_llms_llm_use llms_gemini
Representative_Docs: ['personalize learning llms can be used to generate personalized learning materials for each student promote critical thinking llms can be used to help students develop critical thinking skills by analyzing and evaluating the text that they generate teach students about ai llms can be used to teach students about ai and how it works everyone is parroting these points and i just don t see it you have to input so much information to a llm to get personalized content for a student that it s faster to just do it yourself the lack of live browsing capability means that the llms have severely limited application for classroom tasks with real world connections what advantage does using llms for critical thinking have over using actual real world artifacts yes we can use ai and llms to teach students about ai interested to hear the talk when i have time but i m not hopeful for anything other than the same silly talking points that have been plastered everywhere for the last months', 'i understand the way llms operate well partly because i don t live under a rock but i have also spent a lot of time using it and i ve seen a lot of it in my courses as for use in the classroom the short answer is yes but i also know from my work that generic unedited ai writing consistently scores as very difficult for most people to read and comprehend based on fleshing kincaid gunning fog and every other established measure for the reading level of a given text this does worry me in just how accessible all these possible revolutionary uses for are most students reading and literary skills have already been in decline and i know that a lot of my students in comp i and ii at an open enrollment cc don t have the skills to be able to use ai meaningfully yet the thing that annoys the shit out of me about the white paper you linked and most current ai discussion on par with it is that it all more or less boils down to there are so many possibilities but none of the suggestions are very concrete or actually useful i use ai pretty much every day for various things but none of those things are any of the things the white paper suggests those things are all just here s a thing that you can do that you probably don t really need or want to do but chatgpt can do it even though you don t have any use for it we still haven t figured out the best ways for students or faculty to use it really here s the thing that i can t stand about articles like this though it is working on an assumption that all students are honest and care about the integrity of their work it implies that if we just teach them how to use ai responsibly instead of discouraging it and penalizing it then they will magically use it for all these amazing things here s my experience i adjunct as a side hustle at a school where we cannot penalize or discuss suspicions of ai use with student because the powers that be are operating on this same bullshit assumption of well we can t accuse students without proof and we can t stop them from doing it so let s just encourage critical thinking and honest learning it has been a complete policy failure ai use is fucking rampant easily of students are using ai for every discussion every reply every essay and they re not even trying to hide it they don t even realize that their discussion post is a regurgitated version of about seven other students posts they make for the most boring and tedious discussions but students are also using ai and posting things that are just so wildly unaware of what s going on i ve had students reply to my posts and try to explain basic writing concepts to me a writing professor because they can t even be bothered to read what i actually asked them in the post or what they re copy pasting out of chatgpt just as an example one of the discussion in the blueprint i teach asks them to evaluate one sample essay from a list of three options in their original psot and then reply to others this happened multiple times per class student a posts a generic ai generated evaluation of essay with vague comments full random writing buzzwords student b posts about essay and student a responds with i also evaluated essay i agree with xyz vague comment random writing buzzwords they don t even realize it s a different essay than theirs student c posts about essay and student a responds with i also evaluated essay i agree with xyz vague comment random writing buzzwords they don t notice that they ve now claimed to have evaluated a different essay in every reply with the same comments in every single one they don t even know what they re saying or what anybody else is saying and they re completely unconcerned with it so maybe i m jaded because of my side gig s complete mishandling of ai but i can only meet these support students don t be adversarial avoid punitive policies suggestions with skepticism we would be foolish to ignore that some students do not give a shit about the integrity of their learning some students just don t want to write a paper and they know they re not going to get in very much trouble with ai and all the ai literacy in the world isn t going to keep them from doing it some students only can be deterred with the possibility of a penalty in my opinion we re all just going to have to be in the trenches for several years before there will be anything that revolutionary to discuss and apply in terms of classroom application for incoming student as well as figure out the best practices for mitigating the integrity issues', 'hey gemini why are academics freaking out about using llms to complete coursework and research long boring answer about intellectual credibility and honesty and nods off waiting for the chat box to finish populating so wait what about glasses let s say there was a guild of the perfectly sighted yeah and nobody else could see so good and the perfect sighted folks got to make all the decisions and catch all the balls confusing answer where gemini tries to tell me a story about a wolf that cried little boy and couldn t derive square roots on paper and failed to become president wtf no i accidentally hit return instead of shift return you effing idiot machine let me finish yeah carefully hits shift return so one day some one invents glasses and the world rejoices because now everyone can see as well as the perfectly sighted guildies you get it instead of the game being all about eyes now the game is about who s the best catch the future of academia glasses for everyone but the game relies on the player the rise of large language models llms has some academics worried fearing these glasses granting everyone access to information will diminish their value however the analogy breaks down unlike eyesight knowledge is constantly evolving llms need continuous updates where public access with diverse perspectives becomes an advantage the true differentiator lies not in having the glasses llms but in how well you play the game conduct research critical thinking becomes paramount can you analyze llm outputs identify biases and formulate novel research questions that these ai assistants might miss furthermore weaving a compelling narrative from the information gathered is crucial just like baseball academia will see a rise in specialization a biochemist and a literary scholar might both use llms but their critical thinking skills will differ the future likely involves collaboration with academics and llms working together each leveraging their strengths in essence llms democratize access to information but success hinges on human strengths critical thinking unique perspectives and the ability to interpret and communicate effectively the future of research lies not in everyone having the same glasses but in how each player uses them to contribute their unique perspective to the game of knowledge creation gemini sonorous rolling tones we gather here today to mourn the passing of yesacadem grindapaperich a a b s m s c i a ph d d n a p b s yesacadem was best known for his strong principled stance against slide rules calculators word processors spell check and the internet who among us does not remember his compelling call to arms do it the hard way or that golden oldie why not just let computers read to us and forget how to think blind dude from close up front in full academic regalia robes sashes impressively tall hat careful now my brother sonorously clears throat and continues or grindy s truly spectacular and multitudinous efforts to define the negative space of knowledge in his field grindy could pump out three peer reviewed articles before breakfast in cuneiform such scholarship i ve heard it said that only three people in all the world can understand the true depths of grindy s work blind dude bangs his staff of knowledge on floor peers around the sub what gemini stole my bit about glasses and games jean luc picard voice computer i need to fight klingons teach me how to manually fly the enterprise students are going to use llms they are using llms and the boring students are all going to use the llms in pretty much the same way boringly professionals are going to use llms professionals are using llm s professionals are not using llm s in all the same boring ways because knowledge is not actually the true heft professionals bring to the game anymore professionals are using creative expertise critical thinking analysis and yes specialized knowledge but in general knowledge is the llm s job now gemini knows more than any one person ever will llms learn differently knowledge is now copied disseminated differently learned differently interacted with differently professors will adapt and begin more closely identifying and training the heft of creative intellect instead of having to instruct students in the various forms points at all those templates in microsoft word and excel it s not like we can t still cook over open fires and make stone tools we have just realized we don t need quite so many people trained to do that as did the ancient egyptians']

Name: 189_coworkers_feel guilty_saved company_clients
Representative_Docs: ['continue life you have another problem the data that your coworkers enter into chatgpt is often added to the training set this means that you can leak your clients proprietary information if you put it into chatgpt and you are correct ai is not intelligent it is a useful tool sometimes but it hallucinates answers and it always sounds like it knows what it is talking about often being confidently dead wrong ai right now is nothing but really really advanced auto complete it doesn t think it is not conscious it is a statistical technique congratulations on the promotion you did not torpedo your coworkers they spoke out of ignorance and it was recognized by the bosses you may have saved the company this is the kind of crap that loses clients', 'you did nothing wrong i d say you didn t even get them in trouble they did it to themselves your manager asked a prompting question and then witnessed the senior employees admit to using ai double down on ai being correct without even questioning what it told them belittle you for having a different opinion and then get proven wrong they confidently admitted to the supervisor that they were giving clients the wrong information and never bothered to double check that information themselves they re using a tool they don t fully understand an over reliance on the technology is the correct take away from your meeting clients should be able to trust the information they re given by consultants the senior employees are risking their personal reputations and the reputation of the business your boss is doing exactly what he s supposed to do follow up on the problem and correct it the senior employees aren t doing their jobs properly and therefore need the extra training the use of ai can can be seen as an ethical dilemma and workplaces need to be safe from bullying even if you didn t take it peraonally an ethics class makes sense to me you don t need to apologize to them they exposed themselves just keep doing you', 'hi during a meeting like two weeks ago my manager brought up the topic of ai in the workplace i said that while i found it a great tool i felt that we should be careful when using it while talking with clients we are a consulting company because when i tried to use it chatgpt often gave oversimplistic or outright wrong answers to more complicated problems regarding a type of small company that are my most frequent clients i knew that some of the senior employees used it but i honestly didn t know they would take offense to what i said i swear one of my older coworkers laughed a bit and said that i should stop being paranoid and cited a case where she talked to a client that wanted an specific information about accounting she s a specialist in marketing and she only managed Name: 193_perusall_annotation_annotations_comments
Representative_Docs: ['i m also a teacher using perusall and i ve run into a slightly different issue than the ai generated comments i have a related question does perusall give instructors any visibility into who s highlighting and copying large portions of the text i ve noticed that some students might be copying whole paragraphs or even entire chapters just to paste into an ai tool outside of perusall the ai generated comments issue is one thing but i m wondering if there s a way to actually track or detect this kind of mass copy and paste behavior on the platform has anyone seen a setting log or report in perusall that shows how much text each student is highlighting or copying or is it basically invisible on the instructor side this is my first semester using it for my class', 'as far as i know the tools that are available for social annotation are perusall and hypothesis neither one currently has any automatic ai detection though perusall does flag comments with pasted text for review in the audit area of the instructor dashboard there are other discussion platforms available like packback and yellowdig but i ve never used them and i m not sure if they have an option to have text or video for students to directly comment on perusall does have a feature to add a quiz to an assignment so i ve done that a couple times to help reinforce the reading i shift the grading rubric so that students have to get at least most of the quiz questions right to get full points for the assignment the quiz questions aren t ai proof nothing really is but it s something i don t use perusall for the whole text i just have it for a few assignments for my online class where i really want students to read the book i used an oer that has embedded review quizzes for each section the students need to complete the quiz in order to get credit for the reading and even with that i d bet at least a couple students plugged the quiz questions into chatgpt to get answers rather than reading honestly short of having students submit a hand written reflection or do an oral report on the topic the students who want to use ai as a shortcut will use it no matter what we do i know a colleague who explicitly tells students that exams will contain content that is in the text but wasn t discussed in class so they need to engage with the text if they re going to do well in the course they still have students who skip the readings or default to studying from ai summaries', 'i d like to hear from anyone who s used perusall as a big part of their asynchronous teaching i d also love tips from people who use it to teach humanities and literature in my specific case i am going to try to use perusall as a replacement for class in my asynchronous courses i d like to give students a weekly perusall assignment where most of our activities not just reading take place within the reading assignment equivalent to what i would do during class on campus my goals are to not grade ai drivel to teach active reading and for students to feel they ve participated in benefitted from an interactive experience i m through my first annotation of one novel that i teach i have found opportunities to use the poll anonymous and have to answer before seeing other comments modes of annotation and i m excited about how those can serve class like purposes but most of my annotations are expository glossing vocabulary reminding students of connections to other readings or reflection prompts has this ever happened to you do you agree with the character i don t expect or really want any responses to those annotations i m adding them more like speed bumps to keep students checked in to reading and partly to demonstrate to students the kind of annotations they can make too i ve never seen or participated in an actual perusall text with other readers though and i think i d annotate quite differently if it were a textbook chapter instead of fiction i m not sure i m on the right track is what i m doing likely to be productive pedagogically are students going to engage with my different prompts and annotations do you get better participation with some annotation types vs others can you share a perusall activity within the reading that you think would work well has anyone experienced admin or accreditation push back about whether this counts as rsi for contact hours']

Name: 194_python_language_programming_course
Representative_Docs: ['i get what you mean but i learn a language on codecademy create a project usually a calculator or something a bit more advanced like a game with text inputs since i don t know design then i go to the other language and do the same learning languages these days is really easy since everything is online my goal is to know as many languages as i can learn not to show off but to use them in one big project or maybe use them later anything i learn now will be useful later if i get a job in the beginning that doesn t relate to machine learning but i m currently learning machine learning so i ve stopped jumping around for a while but when i m done with machine learning i m planning on a big project which will basically involve predicting car accidents which includes google libraries and stuff after that i ll get into something like c then c then java thanks for your suggestion though appreciate it', 'what to do next after learning python basics is an often asked question searching for what next on r learnpython gives you too many results here s some wonderful articles on this topic i know how to program but i don t know what to program things you might encounter in your programming journey techniques for efficiently learning programming languages exercises and projects i do not have a simple answer to this question either if you feel comfortable with programming basics and python syntax then exercises are a good way to test your knowledge the resource you used to learn python will typically have some sort of exercises so those would be ideal as a first choice i d also suggest using the below resources to improve your skills if you get stuck reread the material related to those topics search online ask for clarifications etc in short make an effort to solve it it is okay to skip some troublesome problems and come back to it later if you have the time but you should be able to solve most of the beginner problems maintaining notes and cheatsheets will help too especially for common mistakes exercism practicepython edabit these are all beginner friendly and difficulty levels are marked page python intro exercises exercises from my introductory guide codewars adventofcode projecteuler more challenging checkio codingame codecombat gaming based challenges r dailyprogrammer not active currently but there s plenty of past challenges with discussions once you are comfortable with basics and syntax the next step is projects i use a line program that solves a common problem for me adding body text align justify to epub files that are not justify aligned i didn t know that this line would help beforehand found a solution online and then automated the process of unzipping epub adding the line and then packing it again that will likely need you to lookup documentation and go through some stackoverflow q amp a as well and once you have written the solution and use it regularly you ll likely encounter corner cases and features to be added i feel this is a great way to learn and understand programming practice python projects my book on beginner to intermediate level projects projects with solutions algorithms data structures networking security databases etc project based learning web applications bots data science machine learning etc pytudes by peter norvig python programs usually short of considerable difficulty books the big book of small python projects tiny python projects impractical python projects and real world python r learnpython what do you automate with python at home debugging knowing how to debug your programs is crucial and should be ideally taught right from the beginning instead of a chapter at the end of the book think python is an awesome example for such a resource material sites like pythontutor allow you to visually debug a program you can execute a program step by step and see the current value of variables similar feature is typically provided by ides like pycharm and thonny under the hood these visualizations are using the pdb module see also python debugging with pdb debugging is often a frustrating experience taking a break helps and sometimes i find the solution or spot a problem in my dreams try to reduce the code as much as possible so that you are left with minimal code necessary to reproduce the issue talking about the problem to a friend colleague inanimate objects etc can help too known as rubber duck debugging i have often found the issue while formulating a question to be asked on forums like stackoverflow reddit because writing down your problem is another way to bring clarity than just having a vague idea in your mind here s some more articles on this challenging topic what does debugging a program look like how to debug small programs debugging guide problem solving skills here s an interesting snippet paraphrased from a collection of interesting bug stories gt a jpeg parser choked whenever the ceo came into the room because he always had a shirt with a square pattern on it which triggered some special case of contrast and block boundary algorithms see also this curated list of absurd software bug stories testing another crucial aspect in the programming journey is knowing how to write tests in bigger projects usually there are separate engineers often in much larger number than code developers to test the code even in those cases writing a few sanity test cases yourself can help you develop faster knowing that the changes aren t breaking basic functionality there s no single consensus on test methodologies there is unit testing integration testing test driven development and so on often a combination of these is used these days machine learning is also being considered to reduce the testing time see testing firefox more efficiently with machine learning for example when i start a project i usually try to write the programs incrementally say i need to iterate over files from a directory i will make sure that portion is working usually with print statements then add another feature say file reading and test that and so on this reduces the burden of testing a large program at once at the end and depending upon the nature of the program i ll add a few sanity tests at the end for example for my command help project i copy pasted a few test runs of the program with different options and arguments into a separate file and wrote a program to perform these tests programmatically whenever the source code is modified for non trivial projects you ll usually end up needing frameworks like built in module unittest or third party modules like pytest here s some learning resources getting started with testing in python python testing style guide calmcode pytest tdd in python with pytest obeythetestinggoat tdd for the web with python selenium django javascript and pals modern test driven development in python tdd guide has a real world application example intermediate to advanced python resources official python docs python docs are a treasure trove of information calmcode videos on testing code style args kwargs data science etc practical python programming covers foundational aspects of python programming with an emphasis on script writing data manipulation and program organization beyond the basic stuff with python best practices tools and techniques oop practice projects fluent python takes you through python s core language features and libraries and shows you how to make your code shorter faster and more readable at the same time serious python deployment scalability testing and more practices of the python pro learn to design professional level clean easily maintainable software at scale includes examples for software development best practices algorithms and design patterns problem solving with algorithms and data structures classic computer science problems in python deepens your knowledge of problem solving techniques from the realm of computer science by challenging you with time tested scenarios exercises and algorithms github collection of design patterns and idioms architecture patterns with python enabling tdd ddd and event driven microservices handy cheatsheets python crash course cheatsheet comprehensive python cheatsheet scientific python cheatsheet common beginner errors use the pdf link data structures and algorithms in python this course is taught by prof madhavan mukund of chennai mathematical institute i like to thank the whole team of this course for making such a great course on such an unfamous topic and providing such great quality at free of cost who should do this course and why it s a great course for beginners in programming because it teaches python from basic to intermediate level if you don t have any prior knowledge of programming or python you can take this course no prerequisites also this course teaches search and sorting algorithms at a very deep level it also has many data structures like linked list binary tree heap stack and queue but i would say the algorithms part was better the data structure part was taught at a very basic level i would recommend studying more about it from other resources good luck if you are starting this course why python can be a great programming language for learning data structure and algorithms python has a very beginner friendly and easy to remember syntax python also has many inbuilt functions which can be very useful because you don t have to write the basic function in your code again and again like you have to do in c or import any library most of the time but python hides data types of variables because it s a dynamically typed language python is a very popular language for machine learning scientific calculations and data science python is an interpreted language no need to compile the code python is very easy to set up so choose according to your aspirations and needs in the last Name: 195_tt_teaching_tenure_faculty
Representative_Docs: ['any thoughts on how to get out of teaching while staying in higher ed my university i m a woman tenure track faculty member at a private r in the humanities think english literature philosophy history etc i m struggling with deep discouragement and dislike of teaching in this era of increasing grade grubbing accomodations requests chatgpt and the pressure from course evals to keep the students feeling good about themselves regardless of whether they did any work etc this week felt horrible in terms of stress as i was just completely buried under special testing requests grading of essays that may or may not have been written by humans students miffed because i didn t give them a study guide detailing exactly what terms and quotes to study for the final difficult office hours meetings with students unhappy because i wasn t agreeing to let them make up x or y assignment from september ugh i don t see the dynamic getting any better since my uni just approved a whole student personal days policy and everything all the university level movement seems to be towards giving more reign to student demands rather than less in the next years how could i shift to some kind of more administrative or staff role i know that i probabably need to stick it out another years and get tenure since to be tenure track and then not seek or get tenure would surely invite some stigma and close doors but assuming i do what kinds of other roles could i shift into at the university and how does one do that what comes to mind is a staying faculty but doing departmental administrative work our chair and various program directors get course releases so this may be one way to teach less i know the dept admins deal with a lot of student complaints up the chain which could be depressing but it seems anything would be better than the viscerality and vulnerability of being on the front lines so much myself i really struggle with the exhaustion of teaching and struggle not take personally the course evals pettiness cheating attempts constant demands from students to give exceptions re dos etc i do think i have colleagues who enjoy teaching more than i do so could i do more good on the back end of department admin trying to support them in holding the line and be myself happier but i would still have to teach some classes so it would just be a lessening of student contact if i went this way and i am not sure realistically how soon i could get into a role that would make a tangible difference in reduced teaching although of course all the lingo around the university is about equity and representation and whatnot the reality is that my department a couple dozen full time faculty has never had a woman chair and or a minority chair so i actually don t know if i could get such a leadership role it is not that long ago that these admin roles were always white male priests in particular catholic university in general i think i am well liked and considered competent so i think i could eventually end up in some kind of department or program leadership but the problem is that it would take a while and i m not sure i can survive the stress of full time teaching for too many more years i m extremely tired and admittedly part of that is because of my own family circumstances of being mom to several small children at home and not really sure that i can face another or or years of day in day out teaching and student demands requiring me to do my job multiple times but even if i could get into the program admin roles that come with course releases that s not going to happen overnight b shifting to some kind of non direct student suppor staff role on campus overall our university staff seem to have much better quality of life in that they have all the university benefits like tuition remission but depending on their roles not having to be on the receiving end of so much teaching stress and student haggling students have much chummier relationships with the student support and accomodations office and dei staff on campus since they are seen as friendly advocates for success while the faculty no matter how much we bend over backwards to give students what they want are seen as the enemy holding their a hostage so though i resent somewhat how this staff faculty dynamic plays out in student culture maybe if you can t fight them you join em if i switch to the staff side would life be more pleasant with students or at least more distanced from students unfortunately i suspect it wouldn t make sense for me to try to shift into the kinds of academic support staff roles that i undersatnd and see most on the day to day like offices of student support dei accomodations pedagogy teaching learning office admittedly part of the mis fit is me since i ve developed a sort of internal beef about them in my perception undermining student perceptions of faculty and hijacking the process of students taking responsibility for their own learning it s not that i don t care about diversity or compassion since questions of genuine inclusion are actually very important to me personally and in my research i study history of immigrant religious populations religious and ethnic minorities etc but it s just that i have gotten jaded these last couple years after seeing how the campus culture of the student customer is always right has in practice made my life as in fact a pretty vulnerable female minority faculty member swimming upstream in a lot of ways more hellish and difficult none of the campus discussion of mental health accomodations for students has helped my mental health as a faculty member pulling all nighters responding to demanding student emails for special requests none of the university pedagogy teaching institute people urging us to reflect on our course evals every term so we can do better seem to themselves face the psychological onslaught of having to read whatever year olds might decide to write about them during the most stressful end of term weeks when they are resentful about assignments and worried about grades none of the dei office people has ever actually advocate for policy changes that would actually help me as a woman minority mother faculty member like being able to use start up research funds for summer childcare so i can get research done sorry for the rant obviously i don t express this stuff out on campus but my point is that i am so discouraged or cynical about what i consider the hypocrisy of these student support type offices not really supporting good teaching and learning that it probably rules out a chunk of staff roles for me i would not be a good fit so what are the other more faculty adjacent type roles that i could shift into my sense is there are a lot of director or associate director of x office people or program coordinator of z center people floating around campus probably earning higher or comparable salaries as i do though i don t know exactly what most of them do or whether i could realistically pivot to get those kinds of jobs have you ever seen a faculty member be able to shift to some kind of staff role that wasn t teaching but really did seem to support the education of the place how did they get those jobs what enabled them to turn their very faculty oriented cvs into viable candidates for those good staff jobs i know some people will just say go to industry but very practically i don t see a way or much hope in getting out of academia entirely since a i m in the humanities social sciences and b financially i am still hoping my children could get tuition remission or exchange down the line we are barely affording basic living costs since we ve faced these terribly high rents and home mortgage prices of the last few years in settling down recently so since my salary is pretty low and we have not been able to save for retirement or for the kids college without a better paying industry to go to i think my best bet to keep supporting the family is still to somehow stay in higher ed and hope for that tuition remission exchange to work when my kids are old enough to go to college ps i m sorry for the selfishness of this question i went into academia wanting to be a good teacher and to make a difference educating students so i m sad ashamed to have gotten so cynical about university life that i am thinking in such self defensive ways about trying to just minimize my vulnerability to student haggling while keeping a job but i m feeling pretty hopeless since i thought teaching would be better than it is and just cannot see any light at the end of any tunnel in faculty life these days what s my next move i d really appreciate any examples of non or minimal Name: 196_podcast_lex_lex fridman_fridman
Representative_Docs: ['talking machines is an excellent podcast on machine learning you don t need a lot of background to understand it and it is very neatly made and interesting if you have any interest in the topic the effort report is a podcast on academia from prof roger peng and prof elizabeth matsui it s very casual and feels more like a coffee machine chat than a directed discussion but both hosts are very interesting people and make it worth the listen not so standard deviations is a podcast by prof roger peng and dr hilary parker on diverse news related to data analysis and data science research in action is a podcast about academia by dr katie linder and is a lot more focused usually tackling one specific topic related to the guest she s interviewing daniel and jorge explain the universe is a science related podcast by prof daniel whiteson and dr jorge cham the phdcomics artist mit artificial intelligence podcast is a podcast focused on ai stuff by dr lex fridman usually focused on one topic with a guest covering it', 'lex fridman artifical intelligence podcast hands down top podcast for being obsessed for ai', 'artificial intelligence with lex fridman is an amazing podcast also software engineering daily is pretty good']

Name: 197_assignments_ai resistant_resistant_ai proof
Representative_Docs: ['i think writing assignments that are now trivially easy to generate with ai have to be done away with entirely unless it s done in class under supervision ai is a good tutor and has helped me understand many things even it it can make mistakes so i think we need to rethink our assignments from the ground up to fit the world we live in and not just bemoan that the old ways don t work anymore and hope ai just somehow goes away', 'so you ve got an ai product to grade essays you can t ethically penalize a student for letting ai doing their job if you re gonna let it do yours ai can make assignments grade these assignments can probably create lectures and modules for courses yes they are lower quality than human doing the work but it s way cheaper so thanks for moving the ball forward in the we don t need professors any more game who cares if they learn anything right why bother teaching them how to think as long as the complete tasks', 'you mentioned best practices for making your assignments ai resistant could you please share some']

Name: 198_music_movies_midi_audio
Representative_Docs: ['there are a few choices you have getting started firstly are you allowed to use existing machine learning libraries and frameworks or are you expected to interpret and build the algorithms yourself i will assume it is the first case otherwise you will need a much deeper understanding and considerably more time to achieve this goal amp x b machine learning itself is a huge a field so to help narrow down your search a little there are a couple of families of approaches within machine learning which i would recommend looking into unsupervised learning this approach focuses on grouping similar items together by comparing similarities in your case you could use your questionnaire answers to group people for example people who watch movies like the notebook and the fault in our stars could be grouped by into a romantic drama category a good algorithm to get started with here is k nearest neighbour supervised learning this approach tries to categorise items into predefined categories and learns from a data set which has example items labelled with which category they belong in in your case you could have one question in your questionnaire be what are your favourite categories of movies this would be the labels for that persons data set then any movies which they list they like can be associated with those categories a good starting place here would be to look in artificial neural networks amp x b whichever machine learning approach you choose you will need data to train and test your network this has to be a pretty large data set if you want to get any good results and is probably going to be your biggest barrier to trying to achieve this the suggested approaches for your problem are also simplified and won t necessarily be great ways to categorise your data amp x b if you are allowed to use existing machine learning libraries and frameworks here are a few i would recommend they can all implement the unsupervised and supervised learning methods mentioned above keras a high level machine learning library with good support built of google s tensorflow tensorflow the foundation of keras allows more in depth tweaking and control if you need a more bespoke solution azure this is microsofts machine learning offering i ve not used it myself but i ve heard good things amp x b let me know if you have any more specific questions', 'sounds like it is an assignment for you either from some software development company or a final project of sort if it is one of these and you are only now starting out then you will not get it done in time but if it is your own project or something that interests you then you have all your time to learn these topics concepts i will list then things you should know to implement this kind of system data mining you will have a whole set of data either from some database api or it is gathered on event basis in that data there are certain properties that you are using to group up and analyze these movies title summary the name of author the producer etc if it is slowly gathered on event basis then perhaps the movies that a person x is picking to his lending cart or such similarities can be used to profile both the people lending movies and also to profile the movies itself machine learning you will be automating the process of data mining and making the algorithm learn from its own output i have dealt much less with machine learning so my description here will be short database management you will be storing the data in some database so you should know how to set up its structure schema or such how to make queries on it etc you will need either some sql or a knowledge in doing nosql queries some back end programming language and its framework you will be programming the points and also you will be processing queries on the existing dataset that come from users perhaps microservices most likely you will want to keep your code in separate modules one for processing incoming queries for movie recommendations one for mining the data maybe one for machine learning so a knowledge in microservices will be needed but you can do it in one monolith as well and then skip the point if you are using microservices then there will be a communication between these services for communication you will need then some message queuing technology may it be kafka rabbitmq or some other mq personally i would pick kafka but again if you are going for monolith then you can skip point some front end framework react vue angular you will wish to build the interface web page for the user so there has to be that knowledge in web application security so people can t steal the data you have analyzed can t steal users data if there are user accounts can t fool the data mining and machine learning algorithms with fake corrupt data can t take down your web page or modify it etc', 'firstly let me say quite clearly that i am absolutely not a music person i took saxophone lessons for about a year or so when i was like in rd or th grade around years ago and that s it so whatever i say here is through the eyes of someone with a complete lack of formal music education that being said how do we define music is a bunch of pots and pans falling off a shelf and making a loud noise music is beethoven s th symphony music is a three month old baby crying music these may seem like very random examples and only one of them is pretty universally defined as music but what mathematically is the difference there are certainly some pieces that can be mathematically defined the relationships between notes in a chord or the relative pitches in a harmony or musical tempos are all relatively easy to assign numbers to one possible solution instead of trying to code out every single possible and highly subjective rule for what defines music is to have the program figure it out on its own you could use machine learning here to analyze existing music samples and attempt to let the program determine what the musical patterns are for example a simple markov chain fed midi data could potentially produce its own music take a sample melody subdivide it into a list array of notes for each note or group of notes create an entry in a frequency list for each frequency entry determine how often that particular entry is followed by another entry so how often is bb followed by g how often is it followed by ab reverse the process pick a starting note let s say bb and using the frequency array for that note pick a weighted random following note keep picking notes until you have enough however there s still a problem of definition even here notice i say midi data because midis specifically record notes and times instead of raw audio data with the former you have atomic integral note data that you can analyze and manipulate with the latter you d have to specifically analyze waveforms which should be a lot more difficult less productive secondly notice that i say note or group of notes this is because the actual importance of bb is followed by g here may be negligible in other words the fact that bb is followed by g of the time and by ab of the time may not be musically meaningful randomly picking a following note after bb may not produce anything that sounds well like music instead it might be helpful to consider larger groups such as chords groups of or more notes or whatever']

Name: 200_glasses_ai glasses_meta_exam
Representative_Docs: ['using meta glasses to cheat on an exam is not a human right again if there is some sort of reason a student needs ai glasses instead of just regular prescription glasses then the onus is on them to reach out to the office of disability to make reasonable accommodations ahead of time perhaps they could be provided with a normal pair of prescription glasses for exam days', 'the display glasses might allow you to do that but the regular meta glasses have no screen so they would have to ask it to solve the problem and it would probably stand out between them speaking and the audio that is hard to hear but not completely silent meta ai is okay but not even close to the best i would not trust it to give anything more than surface level answers i have the regular meta glasses students could take pictures or videos of the test but only if they block the light that shows up when you take a picture or record the display glasses will be obvious as they are a lot larger than the regular glasses if you see someone during a test with those make them take off the neural band if they claim the glasses are prescription as it will make it hard to control without voice control', 'you can t stop students from wearing glasses during an exam even in some hypothetical world where you would ask students to not wear glasses during an exam a bunch of students would get accommodations but it would be difficult to verify that glasses were not actually ai glasses many if not most ai glasses currently require voice commands to provide ai generated content so there s that they re also expensive though not so expensive that students wouldn t buy them i m not sure how much cheating using ai glasses is currently happening my guess is it s far less common than other ai cheating but it makes sense to have it on our radar']

Name: 201_vowel_phonics_words_sound
Representative_Docs: ['i will give you some tips but first i must say do not overwhelm a toddler with rigorous academic study be consistent but stick to only to minutes of planned phonics a day kids are going to learn with time and patients a toddler will become frustrated if forced to sit and practice letters for more than minutes you don t want them to run away or be upset with you when it is time to learn this has to be done with lots of praise and encouragement learning to read is difficult consistency is the best way to teach a child to read find a routine and stick to it here is the routine we used to teach our preschoolers to read do not go past step until your child has mastered letter sounds after this stick to the same step and reviewing previous steps before moving on daily abc practice saying the letter name and sounds abc songs bingo song interactive game board for letter sound recognition read a book a day when they begin to understand blending of cvc word then have them read beginner books like bob books once they have a grasp on identifying letters and their sounds begin to teach cvc words c a t is a cvc word it is a consonant vowel short sound consonant these letter words are easy to read and are a great confidence booster for new readers introduce sight words sight words are high frequency words that appear often in the english language you can always find a list of sight words with a quick internet search i suggest learning these words first i the a it an of on see look at write down short sentences for your child to read these sentences should only include cvc words and the sight words learned introduce cvce words these words have a consonant vowel consonant and silent letter e the letter e makes the vowel in the middle say its name long sound example h u g turns to h u g e and m a d turns to m a d e introduce more sight words introduce starting clusters and ending clusters example sh ch tr sp introduce vowel combinations example ai in maid ee in seed oo in book oi in boil continue to move up in the level of books they read encourage and praise the child as they progress focus on what good they have done learning to read is difficult but it does not mean a parent should not attempt to teach a child how', 'i find this explanation to be a good one digraphs are letters and diphthongs are sounds a digraph is two letters that spell one sound digraphs that spell consonant sounds include the letter pairs sh ch th wh ck ph ng digraphs that spell vowel sounds include the letter pairs ai ay ee ea ie ei oo ou ow oe oo ue ey ay oy oi au aw the important thing to remember is that a digraph is made of two letters and although the letters spell a sound the digraph is the two letters not the sound a diphthong is a special kind of vowel sound most vowel sounds in english are made with the mouth in one position and with one pure sound these vowel sounds are called monophthongs mono meaning one and phthong meaning sound a diphthong is one vowel sound formed by the combination of two vowel sounds a diphthong begins as one vowel sound and moves toward another such as the vowel sounds in coin or loud diphthongs can be contrasted with two monophthong vowel sounds together that form two syllables such as in chaos triage or violin when teaching reading the two vowel sounds most commonly identified as diphthongs are oy and ow the most common spellings for the vowel sound oy are oy toy and oi void and the two most common spellings for ow are ow cow and ou cloud the fact that these two diphthongs are usually spelled with digraphs may explain the confusion between the terms', 'i m not an early childhood educator but i do tutor reading at all ages so i can give a little insight to what you re talking about first off two letter words are almost always going to be sight words because we use them so often that it doesn t even really matter if they follow any rules or not at of or so me we to etc next children learn phonics in stages first letter sounds then it s common to learn words that follow a cvc consonant vowel consonant pattern which are just about always going to have short vowel sounds ex car met then words that follow the cvce pattern with long vowel sounds ex care words that follow the spelling pattern but don t follow the rule ex come some are learned separately as sight words after mastering short and long vowel sounds children learn that some words have pairs of letters that make singular vowel sounds like ai ay making long a or ee ea making long e then they learn that sometimes those same letters make different sounds read read there are many other phonics rules that early readers learn just to then be shown examples of words that don t follow those rules ex owl follows the ow ou sound pattern but own does not this is of course paired with reading out loud to them and encouraging them to sound out words that are sound out able with the rules they know once they see and hear a word enough times it will essentially become a sight word and they can work on decoding more difficult words with more complicated rulesets words like eye are great for introducing outside the context of a passage and instead given as one of a group of words having to do with the body or face for example perhaps with a diagram to show what it is kids might not know how to spell eye immediately but they could probably recognize it from a picture if their parents taught them what eyes are on their body eta tldr my main advice is to keep going with phonics but also know that some words are going to need to be memorized instead of decoded']

learning a i and so on', 'gt no offense but are you sure about this fairly sure but i ve been wrong before gt i don t want to sound in denial i m not i m genuinely trying to learn but a lot of different trends in computer science seem to indicate to me that graphics vision programming will become big in years including alright let s examine them gt rapid advancements in gpus rather than cpus well sure but gpus becoming faster and cheaper does not stimulate demand for graphics software likewise demand for graphics software does not translate into loads of jobs writing low level d code to use a historical analogy over the last eighteen years the number of network connected devices in the world has increased many times over but a sizeable proportion of developers do not spend their days implementing tcp ip stacks network software is commodity software and i suspect a lot of ar vr tech will be similar gt rapid advances in ai which definitely fuels research in computer vision which is at the core of ar vr questionable imo and still doesn t turn into jobs writing either loads of d code or computer vision algorithms both will be commoditised into libraries or even third party services why would i pay a graduate to write their own graphics code when i could pay them to import a library written by a phd and then use the rest of their time scrubbing munging different data sources also you seem to be conflating computer vision and d work which is understandable but i think misguided they have overlapping uses in things like ar but are ultimately very different fields gt major tech giants all dumping money into vision and ar vr there are lots of reasons tech companies make big investments in exciting new technologies they re making a bet they have lots of capital that is better spent on moonshot gambles than sitting in a bank they are scared a competitor will innovate in the space and find some way to steal their lunch investors really like it none of which say much about the growth of ar any more than the late s heavy investment in wearables turned into anything beyond sports watches gt am i really dumb for thinking we re on the cusp of an ar vr revolution no not at all but it might be prudent not to stake your whole career on it if i had to bet i d say the main uses at least for the next few years will be advertising and events i know of a few digital agencies hiring webgl people to do things like product launch websites retail experiences etc look at agencies like eventstag or koffeecup in the uk most of their projects are things like an ar app to imagine owning a jaguar a shell oil interactive demonstrating co emissions during logistics maybe that s the sort of work you re interested in digital agencies don t pay that much though look you seem to be really really interested in ar and vr so much so i think it could be a good choice whatever the industry conditions you don t need there to be hundreds of vr jobs you just need there to be one that fits your skills i m just not convinced after working in the ar vr space myself for two years that either software or hardware are the things preventing user uptake it might remain a niche technology but if it s a real personal interest i think you can overcome that by showing employers your passion and expertise', 'ar vr dev here get familiar with unity and unreal more so unity math wise if you can do linear algebra and can use d math you re good computer vision has a lot of applications in ar when it comes to image object recognition but ar hardware really isn t at a level where this is used heavily yet over the next couple years it will come into more use graphics can be extremely valuable for either in particular being able to write good high performance shaders is in very high demand there s not a lot of crossover with ai and ar vr right now but i could certainly see that happening in the future for example once we have some good wearable ar glasses having an ar assistant that follows you around there is some niche jobs though for example nlp is very useful for voice controls and there s not many tools to support that right now as far as jobs go gaming takes the biggest share right now but as a percent of the market it s declining vr is seeing growing use as a training tool in particular to train on medical equipment procedures and complicated expensive industrial equipment over the next couple years that s likely the direction the industry will continue to go ar is all over the place right now so it s too early to say what it eventually does but that breakthrough will come with wearable computing the best use case i ve personally seen so far i make software that helps with equipment training is in using glasses to essentially provide more information like display a place in the world where someone needs to put an object or unscrew something or press a button doing this really pushes the limits of what the technology can do right now because it requires object recognition a natural input system closely aligning meshes with real world objects and the equipment the current hardware can only scratch the surface of that the best option is a magic leap and it has some significant restrictions not to mention is expensive we re probably years out from what i just described being ready for mass deployment in companies maybe more depending on what the field of view and near plane are like on upcoming hardware one thing i would recommed studying that you probably haven t considered for ar vr and no one in this thread has mentioned yet is multiplayer having networked simulations is huge everyone wants this and if they don t it s only because they don t realize it s possible for example getting a whole surgical team into the same vr simulation and having them work together or building virtual classrooms or building networked ar apps where you can do things like pathfind from your location to a specific person in the real world if you want to get good with ar vr make sure you know how to handle that']

Name: 211_managers_ics_management_manager
Representative_Docs: ['are managers getting laid off as much as ics is it equally hard or harder to get a manager job now also does ai automation impact managers as much asking as an ic with no people management experience', 'people do effectively what you re saying just not in those terms the way sophisticated people build teams today is with much smaller flatter teams it s not about replacing management or replacing ics it s about merging those two jobs into one thing there is a person in the hierarchy where the manager would have been but that person just owns implementing the whole process end to end themself working as an ic instead of hiring whether that s a manager who replaced ics with ai or an ic who replaced management with ai isn t really a meaningful way of framing what s happening there it s both and neither it s just a different way of organizing a team entirely', 'every time i see people mention it it always seems like a joke however when you think about it it makes more sense than replacing ics think about it why do we have so many layers of management in an organization it s because one person realistically can t keep track of so many people reporting information to them so instead they have managers report to them all the way up the chain this is where ai comes in instead of ics reporting to managers they just all report to the ai hell the ai doesn t even need to be reported to because it already knows what everyone has been doing due to monitoring everyone s computers all the ceo or board of directors needs to do is ask for updates from the ai they can get very detailed information or high level overviews no more time wasted on useless on s you just ask the ai how you could do better or the ai will automatically give you feedback or put you on pip if needed based on a standard set of criteria so no bias that solves one problem that is faced by large organizations but how about another one think about all the time spent in meetings between managers to only come up with stupid decisions because normally the loudest voice will just win out and it isn t always the smartest instead the ai can interact directly with the smes to assess all the information available and make the most informed decisions think of the time savings in conclusion i think we are headed for a time where mid management will no longer exist a near flat org mostly run by ai will be the most efficient corporate structure and will out compete all of the competition boards of directors will be forced to implement this type of structure because otherwise they will be failing their shareholders thoughts']

uncommon to do something with a database jdbc etc or to use spring web applications annotations etc then you re likely do some more theoretical courses such as discrete math and algorithms data structures beyond cs there s often a computer architecture course and a programming language courses where you look at other programming language paradigms like functional programming low level department is course work might include database artificial intelligence machine learning data science numerical analysis theory of computation advanced algorithms computer graphics computer games hated my coworkers when i was a teacher i don t need to get into it but i was amazed at how bad people were at this job teachers tend to be book smart but astoundingly life stupid education in the us is a feminized environment and i m a man these types of environments don t appreciate gender diversity in my experience they want to limit men coming into their departments as much as possible they of a team with both men and women furthermore i loved that the job was lightly physical i was eventually fired after i reported our management for inappropriate behavior but i still have fond memories of the spa i don t want to get into the specifics of my next job but it was servicing a hot air balloon the balloons are somewhat comparable to boats as you need to work as a team to keep it moored leave after i found them breaking the law but i am expecting a sizeable settlement from this position pros the jobs is outdoors it is technical and social and i would work on certain skills that i ve come to appreciate the job descriptions i ve read list accommodating passengers as a job function and that was one of my favorite parts about hospitality i wouldn t need to go into debt to gain these skills i could either attend a maritime science program one of the community colleges i used to teach for has a respectable one or according to another redditor the employers will train you on the job it s a full time job after i was burned so badly in the teaching world i have come to appreciate anything and everything that is a full time job cons i worry about the schedule i m looking for something that would allow me to have a family life and not something that is going to bring me away from my family for days and weeks on end some deckhand jobs allow you to go home every night so i would focus on that the job posting i ve seen require at least year of relevant experience i worry that even if i do a maritime science degree i won t make the qualification for year of experience i m years old and i don t want to do a lot of bitch work or get yelled at by someone born after this is also why i hesitate to become a paralegal and one reason i decided against joining the us coastguard financial advisor i have a passion for personal finance which i discovered in my adult life after i finished my degree in education i taught myself the fundamentals because again personal finance is not taught in us schools and i discovered that it gave me very real very salient power pros i have found the major three investment brokers charles schwab fidelity and vanguard to be the most competent user friendly and frankly intelligent institutions i have ever interacted with it stood out to me because us schools have been so abhorrent i looked online at for example entry level jobs with fidelity they re full time yay and would require me to acquire some licenses that i believe would only take a few months personal finance is something near and dear to me on a personal level learning the fundamentals as an adult has been life changing and has improved every aspect of my life even outside of my finances it is a passion i am a devoted listener to the clark howard show and have given my friends and family advice to me it would possibly be a way to combine my experience with teaching with my passion for personal finance cons i would be working for a major corporation most likely fidelity my ideal is to work for the government so i d aim for that eventually i think i ve been burned in the past by private companies i don t trust the private sector i worry about sedentary work i am a physical person i enjoy time outdoors i do not want to work in front of a computer all day work life balance is important to me teaching allows me to travel yet i m not so sure this pathway would be as generous in that regard thank you so much for your thoughts', 'hi reddit community this is my first time ever posting on here i am hoping that this thread will reach others felt lost in the career world and was able to find their way my post might be long and come across a bit like a mini novel so please excuse the level of detail just trying to provide a comprehensive picture to help background i am going on years old in high school other than theater and showing excellence in english communication typing really fast i never really had any career aspirations or dreams the only thing i knew is that i wanted to leave the small town i grew up in travel and see the world and hope to find myself along the way i naively thought that just by living life life would just sort of magically deliver the path i was meant to be on ex meeting someone unexpectedly that would change my trajectory meeting someone who would be able to see something in me that i didn t see and guide me in a direction trying out a job and i would meet someone at that job who would then lead me into another job etc my s early s did low wage insurance office jobs hated it and had very little money got my aa degree couldn t choose a major for a ba so i unfortunately let my scholarship go to waste and thought i needed to just live life and explore careers quit my office job to go work on a small cruise line only lasted weeks was working erratic hours on a very small boat in bunk bed style with zero entertainment not even wifi available worked at a little gourmet market doing light food service went back to an insurance office job because it was all i had known and had experience with decided to try flight attending worked internationally but my body was a wreck sleep schedule was always off which made depression and anxiety worse low pay so i felt like i couldn t spend money to go and see the places i was in hard to have a stable relationship when you re away for weeks at a time unhealthy food options after a year of trying that i realized i was already mid s and needed a degree still didn t know what i wanted to pursue so i just chose psychology worked at a nonprofit part time and liked the mission driven work but salaries were dreadful by this time i was approaching late s still lost a friend told me about how she was teaching abroad in s korea got me a job went for a year adventure but then i met someone that person was european and since we were from different countries i wanted to explore that romance so i continued teaching abroad long story short i discovered that teaching abroad allowed me to travel have decent holiday time stability of being in one place and begin to see some small savings for the first time in my life plus i met someone and wanted to pursue that and because i never could figure out a career path in the us it was easier for me to seek teaching contracts abroad in different countries so that one year adventure turned into years s realizing time was of the essence i tried coming back to the us to create a career path lost miracle came through with a contact getting me an ed tech job tried the job for a year but i didn t have enough curriculum knowledge to do well so i had to quit lost again temped for a short while then got a low wage part time job in hospitality at a local hotel obviously not going anywhere and directionless so made the decision to go back abroad spent more years with my partner abroad stably employed making some meager savings again s midlife identity crisis at this point after
